# maaslin2 run
```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(Maaslin2)
library(microbiome)
library(phyloseq)
ps_hm <- readRDS('/home/sonec_iv/RSF_res_4_ps/hm3/RSF_ps_humann_joint.rds')
ps_obj <- ps_hm
otu_matrix <- otu_table(ps_obj)

# Check if it's a matrix
if (is.matrix(otu_matrix)) {
  # Round all numeric values
  otu_matrix[] <- round(otu_matrix)

  # Assign the rounded matrix back to the otu_table
  otu_table(ps_obj) <- otu_table(otu_matrix, taxa_are_rows=taxa_are_rows(otu_table(ps_obj)))
} else {
  stop("otu_table is not a matrix.")
}
#ps_obj <- subset_samples(ps_obj,ps_obj@sam_data$Stage_of_severity_of_periodontitis !='no_data')
#ps_obj <- subset_samples(ps_obj,ps_obj@sam_data$Стадия_тяжести_пародонтита !='средняя, тяжелая')
#ps_obj@sam_data$group <- ps_obj@sam_data$Sex
#ps_obj@otu_table <- ps_obj@otu_table * 10000000
prevalence <- 10/100
#prevalence*100
detection <- 1
taxas <- core_members(ps_obj, detection = detection, prevalence = prevalence, include.lowest = F)
ps_obj <- prune_taxa(taxas, ps_obj)
# Define the fixed effects
#fixed_effects <- c("Stage_of_severity_of_periodontitis")
fixed_effects <- c("Treatment_of_periodontitis")
#fixed_effects <- c("Antibiotics_treatment_in_last_6_months")
# Define the combinations for LM and CPLM
lm_normalizations <- c("NONE", "TSS")
lm_transformations <- c("NONE")
#cplm_normalizations <- c("NONE", "TSS")
#cplm_transformations <- c("NONE")

# Define the combinations for NEGBIN
negbin_normalizations <- c('CSS', 'NONE', 'TMM')
negbin_transformations <- c("NONE")

# List to store fit_data_names and results
fit_data_names <- list()
fit_data_results <- list()

# Transform data to compositional for LM and CPLM
ps_obj_compositional <- microbiome::transform(ps_obj, 'compositional')

# Prepare input data for LM and CPLM
input_data_compositional <- phyloseq::otu_table(ps_obj_compositional) %>%
  data.frame() %>%
  data.frame()
input_metadata <- phyloseq::sample_data(ps_obj_compositional) %>%
  data.frame()

# Loop through all combinations for LM and run MaAslin2
for (normalization in lm_normalizations) {
  for (transformation in lm_transformations) {
    output_dir <- paste0("maaslin2_output_lm_", normalization, "_", transformation)
    fit_data_name <- paste0("fit_data_LM_", normalization, "_", transformation)
    fit_data <- Maaslin2(
      input_data = input_data_compositional,
      input_metadata = input_metadata,
      output = output_dir,
      fixed_effects = fixed_effects,
      # random_effects = "Patient_ID",
      normalization = normalization,
      transform = transformation,
      analysis_method = "LM",
      correction = "BH",
      cores=4
    )
    assign(fit_data_name, fit_data)
    fit_data_names[[length(fit_data_names) + 1]] <- fit_data_name
    fit_data_results[[length(fit_data_results) + 1]] <- fit_data$results
    fit_data_results[[length(fit_data_results)]][["model"]] <- fit_data_name
    print(paste("Completed LM with normalization:", normalization, "and transformation:", transformation))
  }
}

# Loop through all combinations for CPLM and run MaAslin2
for (normalization in cplm_normalizations) {
  for (transformation in cplm_transformations) {
    output_dir <- paste0("maaslin2_output_cplm_", normalization, "_", transformation)
    fit_data_name <- paste0("fit_data_CPLM_", normalization, "_", transformation)
    fit_data <- Maaslin2(
      input_data = input_data_compositional,
      input_metadata = input_metadata,
      output = output_dir,
      fixed_effects = fixed_effects,
      # random_effects = "Patient_ID",
      normalization = normalization,
      transform = transformation,
      analysis_method = "CPLM",
            cores=4,
      correction = "BH"
    )
    assign(fit_data_name, fit_data)
    fit_data_names[[length(fit_data_names) + 1]] <- fit_data_name
    fit_data_results[[length(fit_data_results) + 1]] <- fit_data$results
    fit_data_results[[length(fit_data_results)]][["model"]] <- fit_data_name
    print(paste("Completed CPLM with normalization:", normalization, "and transformation:", transformation))
  }
}

# Prepare input data for NEGBIN (without transformation)
input_data_negbin <- phyloseq::otu_table(ps_obj) %>%
  data.frame() %>%
  data.frame()
input_metadata_negbin <- phyloseq::sample_data(ps_obj) %>%
  data.frame()

# Loop through all combinations for NEGBIN and run MaAslin2
for (normalization in negbin_normalizations) {
  for (transformation in negbin_transformations) {
    output_dir <- paste0("maaslin2_output_negbin_", normalization, "_", transformation)
    fit_data_name <- paste0("fit_data_NEGBIN_", normalization, "_", transformation)
    
    
    capture.output({
      suppressMessages(
        suppressWarnings({
          fit_data <- Maaslin2(
            input_data = input_data_negbin,
            input_metadata = input_metadata_negbin,
            output = output_dir,
            fixed_effects = fixed_effects,
            # random_effects = "Patient_ID",
            normalization = normalization,
            transform = transformation,
            analysis_method = "NEGBIN",
            correction = "BH",
            cores=4
          )
        })
      )
    })
    
    assign(fit_data_name, fit_data)
    fit_data_names[[length(fit_data_names) + 1]] <- fit_data_name
    fit_data_results[[length(fit_data_results) + 1]] <- fit_data$results
    fit_data_results[[length(fit_data_results)]][["model"]] <- fit_data_name
    print(paste("Completed NEGBIN with normalization:", normalization, "and transformation:", transformation))
  }
}

```

# heatmap
```{r, fig.width=15, fig.height=35}
# Load necessary libraries
library(tidyr)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyverse)
library(pheatmap)
library(ggpubr)
# Combine all results into a single data frame, than filter
all_results <- do.call(rbind, fit_data_results)
filtered_results <- all_results %>%
  filter(qval <= 0.05 & abs(coef) >= 1)
filtered_results$effect_size <- filtered_results$coef

# Extract unique metadata values from the 'metadata' column
unique_metadata <- unique(filtered_results$metadata)

# Define a custom color palette
color_palette <- colorRampPalette(c("blue", "white", "red"))(101)

# Initialize a list to store the heatmap data frames
heatmap_data_mas_list <- list()

# Loop through each unique metadata value
for (meta in unique_metadata) {
  # Subset the data for the current metadata
  heatmap_data <- filtered_results %>%
    filter(metadata == !!meta) %>%
    select(feature, model, effect_size) %>%
    pivot_wider(names_from = model, values_from = effect_size) %>%
    column_to_rownames(var = "feature")
  
  # Calculate row-wise mean values to order the rows
  row_means <- rowMeans(heatmap_data, na.rm = TRUE)
  
  # Order the rows based on the mean values
  heatmap_data <- heatmap_data[order(row_means), ]
  heatmap_data_mas <- heatmap_data
  
  # Store the heatmap data frame in the list with the metadata as the name
  heatmap_data_mas_list[[meta]] <- heatmap_data_mas
  
  # Determine the maximum absolute value for color scaling
  max_abs_value <- max(abs(heatmap_data_mas), na.rm = TRUE)
  breaks <- seq(-max_abs_value, max_abs_value, length.out = 101)
  
  # Plot the heatmap
  pheatmap(heatmap_data_mas, 
           cluster_rows = FALSE, 
           cluster_cols = FALSE,
           color = color_palette, 
           breaks = breaks,
           number_color = "black", 
           fontsize_number = 12,
           fontsize=16,
           display_numbers = TRUE, 
           main = paste("Heatmap for Metadata:", meta),
           na_col = "white")  # Ensure NA values are displayed as white
}

# Return the list of heatmap data frames
heatmap_data_mas_list
heatmap_humann <-  pheatmap(heatmap_data_mas, 
           cluster_rows = FALSE, 
           cluster_cols = FALSE,
           color = color_palette, 
           breaks = breaks,
           number_color = "black", 
           fontsize_number = 12,
           fontsize=16,
           display_numbers = TRUE, 
           main = paste("Heatmap for Metadata:", meta),
           na_col = "white")
ggsave('/home/ignatsonets/maaslin2_humann_heatmap.png', dpi=300, width=25, height=15, heatmap_humann)
```

#Maaslin2 barplot for features
```{r, fig.width=13, fig.height=27}
all_results <- do.call(rbind, fit_data_results)
# check heatmap data, than use appropriate model
#results_negbin_tmm <- subset(all_results, all_results$model=='fit_data_NEGBIN_TMM_NONE')
results_negbin_none <- subset(all_results, all_results$model=='fit_data_NEGBIN_NONE_NONE')
#filtered_results <- results_negbin_tmm %>%
#  filter(qval <= 0.05 & abs(coef) >= 1)
filtered_results <- results_negbin_none %>%
  filter(qval <= 0.05 & abs(coef) >= 1)
# remove non-bacterial pathways that snuck into dataframe (no plants/fungi/human etc. in name, still non-bacterial)
filtered_results <- filtered_results[filtered_results$feature !='PWY.821..superpathway.of.sulfur.amino.acid.biosynthesis..Saccharomyces.cerevisiae.', ]
filtered_results <- filtered_results[filtered_results$feature !='PWY66.388..ceramide.degradation.by..alpha..oxidation', ]
filtered_results <- filtered_results[filtered_results$feature !='PWY.8182..valproate..beta..oxidation', ]

filtered_results <- filtered_results[filtered_results$feature !='PWY.6313..serotonin.degradation', ]

filtered_results <- filtered_results %>% mutate(group =
                     case_when(coef > 0  ~ "y", 
                               coef < 0 ~ "n" ))
filtered_results <- filtered_results[order(filtered_results$coef, decreasing = TRUE), ]


filtered_results$Species <- sub(".*\\.s__", "", filtered_results$feature)
write.csv(filtered_results, paste0('/home/ignatsonets/humann_maaslin2_', fixed_effects, '.csv'), row.names = FALSE)
filtered_results$taxa <- filtered_results$Species  # Use species directly for labels
size=20       
# Create the bar plot with ggbarplot
barplot_humann <- ggbarplot(
  filtered_results, 
  #x = "taxa",
  x = 'feature',
  y = 'coef',
  fill = 'group',
  palette = c('n' = "#1BA3C6", 'y' = "#D81B60"),
  sort.by.groups = TRUE,  # Don't sort inside each group
  x.text.angle = 90,
  lab.size =10,
  rotate = TRUE,
  lab.vjust = 0.5,
  width = 0.6,
  position = position_dodge(0.5),
  ylab = FALSE,
  ggtheme = theme_linedraw(),
  #title = 'MaAsLin 2 barplot results for metabolic pathways data assessed with HUMANn\n(fixed_effect=Stage_of_severity_of_periodontitis,\nprevalence=10/100, qval<=0.05, abs(coef)>=1)',
  title = 'Stage_of_severity_of_periodontitis'
) + 
theme(
  axis.text.y = element_text(color = "black", size = size),
  axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1, size = size, color = 'black'),
  legend.position = "nano",
  legend.text = element_text(size = size),
  axis.title.y = element_text(color = "black", size = size, angle = 90),
  axis.title.x = element_text(color = "black", size = size),
  plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = size),
  text = element_text(size = size, colour = 'black')
)
ggsave('/home/ignatsonets/maaslin2_humann_barplot_severity.png', dpi=300, width=25, height=15, barplot_humann)
```

# upd dec 23: new variables
```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(Maaslin2)
library(microbiome)
library(phyloseq)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)
library(ggpubr)

# Load your phyloseq object
ps_hm <- readRDS('/home/sonec_iv/RSF_res_4_ps/hm3/RSF_ps_humann_joint.rds')
ps_obj <- ps_hm

# Check and prepare the data - EXACTLY like your original code
otu_matrix <- otu_table(ps_obj)

if (is.matrix(otu_matrix)) {
  # Round all numeric values
  otu_matrix[] <- round(otu_matrix)
  # Assign the rounded matrix back to the otu_table
  otu_table(ps_obj) <- otu_table(otu_matrix, taxa_are_rows = taxa_are_rows(otu_table(ps_obj)))
} else {
  stop("otu_table is not a matrix.")
}

# Apply prevalence filter
prevalence <- 10/100
detection <- 1/100
taxas <- core_members(ps_obj, detection = detection, prevalence = prevalence, include.lowest = F)
ps_obj <- prune_taxa(taxas, ps_obj)

# Prepare metadata - FIXED: Use data.frame() not as.data.frame()
metadf <- data.frame(sample_data(ps_obj))

# Fix column types
numeric_cols <- c("Age", "Weight", "Height", "BMI")
for(col in numeric_cols) {
  if(col %in% names(metadf)) {
    metadf[[col]] <- as.numeric(metadf[[col]])
  }
}

# Define variables to analyze
excluded_columns <- c("Batch", "No_Patient", "Group", "SampleID", "Timepoint", 
                      "Patient_ID", "Sample_collection_date", "Sample", "ID", "X")

# Get all potential variables
all_vars <- names(metadf)[!names(metadf) %in% excluded_columns]

# Filter variables that have at least 2 unique values
filtered_vars <- character(0)
for(var in all_vars) {
  unique_vals <- unique(metadf[[var]])
  unique_vals <- unique_vals[!is.na(unique_vals)]
  if(length(unique_vals) >= 2 && length(unique_vals) < nrow(metadf) * 0.9) {
    filtered_vars <- c(filtered_vars, var)
  }
}

# Convert filtered variables to factors
for(col in filtered_vars) {
  if(col %in% names(metadf)) {
    metadf[[col]] <- as.factor(metadf[[col]])
  }
}

# Update sample data in phyloseq object
sample_data(ps_obj) <- sample_data(metadf)

# FUNCTION: Run MaAsLin2 with specified parameters
run_maaslin_analysis <- function(input_data, input_metadata, variable, group_name = NULL, 
                                 timepoint = NULL, analysis_method = "LM", 
                                 normalization = "TSS", transformation = "NONE") {
  
  # Create descriptive output directory name
  if(is.null(group_name) && is.null(timepoint)) {
    output_dir <- paste0("maaslin2_", variable, "_", analysis_method, "_", normalization, "_all")
  } else if(!is.null(group_name) && is.null(timepoint)) {
    output_dir <- paste0("maaslin2_", variable, "_", group_name, "_", analysis_method, "_", normalization)
  } else if(!is.null(group_name) && !is.null(timepoint)) {
    output_dir <- paste0("maaslin2_", variable, "_", group_name, "_", timepoint, "_", analysis_method, "_", normalization)
  }
  
  # Convert to data frames (not tibbles)
  input_data_df <- as.data.frame(input_data)
  input_metadata_df <- as.data.frame(input_metadata)
  
  # Clean feature names (remove special characters)
  colnames(input_data_df) <- make.names(colnames(input_data_df))
  
  tryCatch({
    fit_data <- Maaslin2(
      input_data = input_data_df,
      input_metadata = input_metadata_df,
      output = output_dir,
      fixed_effects = variable,
      normalization = normalization,
      transform = transformation,
      analysis_method = analysis_method,
      correction = "BH",
      cores = 4,
      max_significance = 0.1,
      plot_heatmap = FALSE,
      plot_scatter = FALSE
    )
    
    return(fit_data)
    
  }, error = function(e) {
    message(paste("Error in", output_dir, ":", e$message))
    return(NULL)
  })
}

# FUNCTION: Extract and format results
extract_results <- function(fit_data, variable, group_name = NULL, timepoint = NULL, 
                            analysis_method, normalization, transformation) {
  if(is.null(fit_data)) return(NULL)
  
  results <- fit_data$results
  if(nrow(results) == 0) return(NULL)
  
  results$Variable <- variable
  results$Group <- ifelse(is.null(group_name), "All", group_name)
  results$Timepoint <- ifelse(is.null(timepoint), "All", timepoint)
  results$Analysis_Method <- analysis_method
  results$Normalization <- normalization
  results$Transformation <- transformation
  
  # Keep only significant results (q < 0.1)
  sig_results <- results %>% 
    filter(qval < 0.1) %>%
    dplyr::select(feature, Variable, Group, Timepoint, coef, stderr, pval, qval, 
                  Analysis_Method, Normalization, Transformation)
  
  return(sig_results)
}

# MAIN ANALYSIS - SIMPLIFIED AND FIXED
all_combined_results <- list()

# Define groups and timepoints
groups <- c("A", "B", "C")
timepoints <- c("-1", "-2")

# Transform data to compositional for LM and CPLM - EXACTLY like your original code
ps_obj_compositional <- microbiome::transform(ps_obj, 'compositional')

# Prepare input data for LM and CPLM - EXACTLY like your original code
input_data_compositional <- phyloseq::otu_table(ps_obj_compositional) %>%
  as.data.frame()
input_metadata_compositional <- data.frame(sample_data(ps_obj_compositional))

# Prepare input data for NEGBIN - EXACTLY like your original code
input_data_negbin <- phyloseq::otu_table(ps_obj) %>%
  as.data.frame()
input_metadata_negbin <- data.frame(sample_data(ps_obj))

# Define the combinations as in your original code
lm_normalizations <- c("NONE", "TSS")
lm_transformations <- c("NONE")

negbin_normalizations <- c('CSS', 'NONE', 'TMM')
negbin_transformations <- c("NONE")

# For each variable to analyze
for(variable in filtered_vars[1:min(3, length(filtered_vars))]) {  # Test with first 3
  message(paste("\n=== Analyzing variable:", variable, "===\n"))
  
  variable_results <- list()
  
  # 1. Analysis across all groups (A vs B vs C)
  message("1. Running analysis across all groups...")
  
  # Run LM on compositional data
  for(norm in lm_normalizations) {
    for(trans in lm_transformations) {
      message(paste("  LM with", norm, "normalization..."))
      
      fit_lm <- run_maaslin_analysis(
        input_data = input_data_compositional,
        input_metadata = input_metadata_compositional,
        variable = variable,
        analysis_method = "LM",
        normalization = norm,
        transformation = trans
      )
      
      if(!is.null(fit_lm)) {
        res_lm <- extract_results(fit_lm, variable, 
                                  analysis_method = "LM",
                                  normalization = norm,
                                  transformation = trans)
        if(!is.null(res_lm) && nrow(res_lm) > 0) {
          variable_results[[length(variable_results) + 1]] <- res_lm
        }
      }
    }
  }
  # Run NEGBIN on raw data
  for(norm in negbin_normalizations) {
    for(trans in negbin_transformations) {
      message(paste("  NEGBIN with", norm, "normalization..."))
      
      fit_negbin <- run_maaslin_analysis(
        input_data = input_data_negbin,
        input_metadata = input_metadata_negbin,
        variable = variable,
        analysis_method = "NEGBIN",
        normalization = norm,
        transformation = trans
      )
      
      if(!is.null(fit_negbin)) {
        res_negbin <- extract_results(fit_negbin, variable,
                                      analysis_method = "NEGBIN",
                                      normalization = norm,
                                      transformation = trans)
        if(!is.null(res_negbin) && nrow(res_negbin) > 0) {
          variable_results[[length(variable_results) + 1]] <- res_negbin
        }
      }
    }
  }
  
  # 2. Analysis for each group separately
  for(group in groups) {
    message(paste("\n2. Running analysis for Group", group, "..."))
    
    # Get indices for this group
    group_indices <- which(input_metadata_compositional$Group == group)
    
    if(length(group_indices) >= 5) {
      # Subset data for this group
      data_group_comp <- input_data_compositional[, group_indices, drop = FALSE]
      metadata_group_comp <- input_metadata_compositional[group_indices, , drop = FALSE]
      
      data_group_raw <- input_data_negbin[, group_indices, drop = FALSE]
      metadata_group_raw <- input_metadata_negbin[group_indices, , drop = FALSE]
      
      # Run LM for this group
      for(norm in lm_normalizations) {
        for(trans in lm_transformations) {
          message(paste("  LM with", norm, "normalization for Group", group, "..."))
          
          fit_group_lm <- run_maaslin_analysis(
            input_data = data_group_comp,
            input_metadata = metadata_group_comp,
            variable = variable,
            group_name = group,
            analysis_method = "LM",
            normalization = norm,
            transformation = trans
          )
          
          if(!is.null(fit_group_lm)) {
            res_group_lm <- extract_results(fit_group_lm, variable,
                                            group_name = group,
                                            analysis_method = "LM",
                                            normalization = norm,
                                            transformation = trans)
            if(!is.null(res_group_lm) && nrow(res_group_lm) > 0) {
              variable_results[[length(variable_results) + 1]] <- res_group_lm
            }
          }
        }
      }
      
      # Run NEGBIN for this group
      for(norm in negbin_normalizations) {
        for(trans in negbin_transformations) {
          message(paste("  NEGBIN with", norm, "normalization for Group", group, "..."))
          
          fit_group_negbin <- run_maaslin_analysis(
            input_data = data_group_raw,
            input_metadata = metadata_group_raw,
            variable = variable,
            group_name = group,
            analysis_method = "NEGBIN",
            normalization = norm,
            transformation = trans
          )
          
          if(!is.null(fit_group_negbin)) {
            res_group_negbin <- extract_results(fit_group_negbin, variable,
                                                group_name = group,
                                                analysis_method = "NEGBIN",
                                                normalization = norm,
                                                transformation = trans)
            if(!is.null(res_group_negbin) && nrow(res_group_negbin) > 0) {
              variable_results[[length(variable_results) + 1]] <- res_group_negbin
            }
          }
        }
      }
    }
  }
  
  # 3. Analysis for each Group × Timepoint combination
  for(group in groups) {
    for(tp in timepoints) {
      message(paste("\n3. Running analysis for Group", group, "Timepoint", tp, "..."))
      
      # Get indices for this group and timepoint
      comb_indices <- which(input_metadata_compositional$Group == group & 
                            input_metadata_compositional$Timepoint == tp)
      
      if(length(comb_indices) >= 3) {
        # Subset data for this combination
        data_comb_comp <- input_data_compositional[, comb_indices, drop = FALSE]
        metadata_comb_comp <- input_metadata_compositional[comb_indices, , drop = FALSE]
        
        data_comb_raw <- input_data_negbin[, comb_indices, drop = FALSE]
        metadata_comb_raw <- input_metadata_negbin[comb_indices, , drop = FALSE]
        
        # Run LM for this combination
        for(norm in lm_normalizations) {
          for(trans in lm_transformations) {
            message(paste("  LM with", norm, "normalization for Group", group, "TP", tp, "..."))
            
            fit_comb_lm <- run_maaslin_analysis(
              input_data = data_comb_comp,
              input_metadata = metadata_comb_comp,
              variable = variable,
              group_name = group,
              timepoint = tp,
              analysis_method = "LM",
              normalization = norm,
              transformation = trans
            )
            
            if(!is.null(fit_comb_lm)) {
              res_comb_lm <- extract_results(fit_comb_lm, variable,
                                             group_name = group,
                                             timepoint = tp,
                                             analysis_method = "LM",
                                             normalization = norm,
                                             transformation = trans)
              if(!is.null(res_comb_lm) && nrow(res_comb_lm) > 0) {
                variable_results[[length(variable_results) + 1]] <- res_comb_lm
              }
            }
          }
        }
        
        # Run CPLM for this combination
      #  for(norm in cplm_normalizations) {
       #   for(trans in cplm_transformations) {
        #    message(paste("  CPLM with", norm, "normalization for Group", group, "TP", tp, "..."))
            
         #   fit_comb_cplm <- run_maaslin_analysis(
          #    input_data = data_comb_comp,
           #   input_metadata = metadata_comb_comp,
          #    variable = variable,
           #   group_name = group,
          #    timepoint = tp,
           #   analysis_method = "CPLM",
          #    normalization = norm,
          #    transformation = trans
           # )
            
          #  if(!is.null(fit_comb_cplm)) {
           #   res_comb_cplm <- extract_results(fit_comb_cplm, variable,
            #                                   group_name = group,
            #                                   timepoint = tp,
            #                                   analysis_method = "CPLM",
            #                                   normalization = norm,
            #                                   transformation = trans)
            #  if(!is.null(res_comb_cplm) && nrow(res_comb_cplm) > 0) {
            #    variable_results[[length(variable_results) + 1]] <- res_comb_cplm
              #}
            #}
          #}
        #}
        
        # Run NEGBIN for this combination
        for(norm in negbin_normalizations) {
          for(trans in negbin_transformations) {
            message(paste("  NEGBIN with", norm, "normalization for Group", group, "TP", tp, "..."))
            
            fit_comb_negbin <- run_maaslin_analysis(
              input_data = data_comb_raw,
              input_metadata = metadata_comb_raw,
              variable = variable,
              group_name = group,
              timepoint = tp,
              analysis_method = "NEGBIN",
              normalization = norm,
              transformation = trans
            )
            
            if(!is.null(fit_comb_negbin)) {
              res_comb_negbin <- extract_results(fit_comb_negbin, variable,
                                                 group_name = group,
                                                 timepoint = tp,
                                                 analysis_method = "NEGBIN",
                                                 normalization = norm,
                                                 transformation = trans)
              if(!is.null(res_comb_negbin) && nrow(res_comb_negbin) > 0) {
                variable_results[[length(variable_results) + 1]] <- res_comb_negbin
              }
            }
          }
        }
      }
    }
  }
  
  # Combine all results for this variable
  if(length(variable_results) > 0) {
    combined_variable_results <- bind_rows(variable_results)
    all_combined_results[[variable]] <- combined_variable_results
    message(paste("\nTotal significant associations for", variable, ":", nrow(combined_variable_results)))
  } else {
    message(paste("\nNo significant results for", variable))
  }
}

# Save all results
if(length(all_combined_results) > 0) {
  # Save combined results
  all_results_df <- bind_rows(all_combined_results)
  write.csv(all_results_df, "maaslin2_all_results.csv", row.names = FALSE)
  message("\nSaved all results to: maaslin2_all_results.csv")
  
  # Create summary statistics
  summary_stats <- all_results_df %>%
    group_by(Variable, Group, Timepoint, Analysis_Method, Normalization) %>%
    summarise(
      n_significant = n(),
      mean_coef = mean(coef),
      min_qval = min(qval),
      .groups = 'drop'
    )
  
  write.csv(summary_stats, "maaslin2_summary_statistics.csv", row.names = FALSE)
  message("Saved summary statistics to: maaslin2_summary_statistics.csv")
  
  # Create simple summary plot
  if(nrow(summary_stats) > 0) {
    library(ggplot2)
    
    overall_plot <- ggplot(summary_stats, aes(x = Variable, y = n_significant, fill = Analysis_Method)) +
      geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge()) +
      facet_grid(Normalization ~ Group + Timepoint) +
      labs(
        title = "Summary of Significant Associations",
        x = "Variable",
        y = "Number of Significant Features (q < 0.1)",
        fill = "Analysis Method"
      ) +
      theme_minimal() +
      theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
    
    ggsave("maaslin2_overall_summary.pdf", overall_plot, width = 16, height = 10)
    ggsave("maaslin2_overall_summary.png", overall_plot, width = 16, height = 10, dpi = 300)
    message("Saved overall summary plot")
  }
}

# Print completion message
message("\n=== ANALYSIS COMPLETED ===")
message("Generated files:")
message("1. Individual MaAsLin2 output directories for each analysis")
message("2. Combined results: maaslin2_all_results.csv")
message("3. Summary statistics: maaslin2_summary_statistics.csv")
message("4. Overall summary plot (maaslin2_overall_summary.pdf/png)")
```


# upd jan 19: with barplots
```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(Maaslin2)
library(microbiome)
library(phyloseq)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)
library(ggpubr)
library(stringr)

# Create main results directory
main_results_dir <- "/home/sonec_iv/RSF_humann_res"
if (!dir.exists(main_results_dir)) {
  dir.create(main_results_dir, recursive = TRUE)
}
setwd(main_results_dir)

# Load your phyloseq object
ps_hm <- readRDS('/home/sonec_iv/RSF_res_4_ps/hm3/RSF_ps_humann_joint.rds')
ps_obj <- ps_hm

# Check and prepare the data - EXACTLY like your original code
otu_matrix <- otu_table(ps_obj)

if (is.matrix(otu_matrix)) {
  # Round all numeric values
  otu_matrix[] <- round(otu_matrix)
  # Assign the rounded matrix back to the otu_table
  otu_table(ps_obj) <- otu_table(otu_matrix, taxa_are_rows = taxa_are_rows(otu_table(ps_obj)))
} else {
  stop("otu_table is not a matrix.")
}

# Apply prevalence filter
prevalence <- 10/100
detection <- 1/100
taxas <- core_members(ps_obj, detection = detection, prevalence = prevalence, include.lowest = F)
ps_obj <- prune_taxa(taxas, ps_obj)

# Prepare metadata - FIXED: Use data.frame() not as.data.frame()
metadf <- data.frame(sample_data(ps_obj))

# Fix column types
numeric_cols <- c("Age", "Weight", "Height", "BMI")
for(col in numeric_cols) {
  if(col %in% names(metadf)) {
    metadf[[col]] <- as.numeric(metadf[[col]])
  }
}

# Define variables to analyze
excluded_columns <- c("Batch", "No_Patient", "Group", "SampleID", "Timepoint", 
                      "Patient_ID", "Sample_collection_date", "Sample", "ID", "X")

# Get all potential variables
all_vars <- names(metadf)[!names(metadf) %in% excluded_columns]

# Filter variables that have at least 2 unique values
filtered_vars <- character(0)
for(var in all_vars) {
  unique_vals <- unique(metadf[[var]])
  unique_vals <- unique_vals[!is.na(unique_vals)]
  if(length(unique_vals) >= 2 && length(unique_vals) < nrow(metadf) * 0.9) {
    filtered_vars <- c(filtered_vars, var)
  }
}

message(paste("\nFound", length(filtered_vars), "variables to analyze:"))
message(paste(filtered_vars, collapse = ", "))

# Convert filtered variables to factors and print variable info
message("\n=== VARIABLE INFORMATION ===")
for(col in filtered_vars) {
  if(col %in% names(metadf)) {
    metadf[[col]] <- as.factor(metadf[[col]])
    # Get variable information
    unique_vals <- unique(metadf[[col]])
    unique_vals <- unique_vals[!is.na(unique_vals)]
    n_levels <- length(unique_vals)
    var_type <- class(metadf[[col]])
    
    message(paste("Variable:", col))
    message(paste("  Type:", var_type))
    message(paste("  Number of levels/unique values:", n_levels))
    if(var_type == "factor" && n_levels <= 10) {
      message(paste("  Levels:", paste(unique_vals, collapse = ", ")))
    } else if(var_type == "numeric") {
      message(paste("  Range:", min(metadf[[col]], na.rm = TRUE), "to", max(metadf[[col]], na.rm = TRUE)))
      message(paste("  Mean:", mean(metadf[[col]], na.rm = TRUE)))
    }
    message(paste("  NAs:", sum(is.na(metadf[[col]]))))
    message("")
  }
}

# Update sample data in phyloseq object
sample_data(ps_obj) <- sample_data(metadf)

# FUNCTION: Create barplot for significant results
create_barplot <- function(results_df, variable, group_name, timepoint, 
                          analysis_method, normalization, transformation) {
  
  # Filter significant results (qval <= 0.05 and |coef| >= 1)
  filtered_results <- results_df %>%
    filter(qval <= 0.05 & abs(coef) >= 1)
  
  if(nrow(filtered_results) == 0) {
    message(paste("No significant results for", variable, "Group", group_name, 
                  "Timepoint", timepoint, "with current thresholds"))
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # Select top 20 features by absolute coefficient value
  filtered_results <- filtered_results %>%
    mutate(abs_coef = abs(coef)) %>%
    arrange(desc(abs_coef)) %>%
    head(20)
  
  # Add group column for coloring
  filtered_results <- filtered_results %>%
    mutate(group = case_when(
      coef > 0 ~ "y", 
      coef < 0 ~ "n"
    ))
  
  # Sort by coefficient for plotting
  filtered_results <- filtered_results %>%
    arrange(coef)
  
  # Create unique short names for display
  filtered_results <- filtered_results %>%
    mutate(
      # Create unique ID for each feature
      feature_id = 1:n(),
      # Truncate long feature names but keep them unique
      feature_short = ifelse(nchar(feature) > 260, 
                            paste0(substr(feature, 1, 257), "..."), 
                            feature),
      # Make sure all short names are unique
      feature_display = make.unique(feature_short, sep = "_")
    )
  
  # Create filename based on parameters
  filename_base <- paste0(variable, "_", group_name, "_", timepoint, "_", 
                         analysis_method, "_", normalization)
  
  # Save filtered results to CSV
  csv_filename <- paste0(filename_base, "_top20_filtered.csv")
  write.csv(filtered_results, file.path(main_results_dir, csv_filename), row.names = FALSE)
  
  # Create barplot
  size <- 10  # Reduced font size for better fit
  
  # Create title based on parameters
  plot_title <- paste0(variable, " (Group ", group_name, ", Timepoint ", timepoint, 
                      ")\n", analysis_method, " with ", normalization, " normalization")
  
  # Calculate dynamic dimensions
  n_features <- nrow(filtered_results)
  plot_width <- max(12, min(20, n_features * 0.7))  # Increased width
  plot_height <- max(8, min(12, n_features * 0.3))
  
  # Create factor with levels in order of coefficient for proper bar ordering
  # Use the unique display names to avoid duplication issues
  filtered_results$feature_display <- factor(filtered_results$feature_display, 
                                            levels = filtered_results$feature_display)
  
  # Create the plot with ggplot directly for better control
  barplot_humann <- ggplot(filtered_results, aes(x = feature_display, y = coef, fill = group)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 0.6, position = position_dodge(0.8)) +  # Increased bar spacing
    scale_fill_manual(values = c('n' = "#1BA3C6", 'y' = "#D81B60")) +
    coord_flip() +  # Flip for horizontal bars with y-axis labels on left
    labs(
      title = plot_title,
      x = "Feature",
      y = "Coefficient"
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      axis.text.y = element_text(color = "black", size = size, hjust = 1),
      axis.text.x = element_text(color = "black", size = size),  # Added x-axis text
      axis.title.y = element_text(color = "black", size = size + 2, margin = margin(r = 10)),
      axis.title.x = element_text(color = "black", size = size + 2, margin = margin(t = 10)),
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = size + 2, face = "bold", margin = margin(b = 15)),
      legend.position = "none",
      panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey90", linewidth = 0.2),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      plot.margin = margin(20, 20, 20, 20)  # Add margin for better spacing
    ) +
    scale_x_discrete(labels = function(x) {
      # Get the original short names (without the _1, _2 suffixes)
      short_names <- filtered_results$feature_short[match(x, filtered_results$feature_display)]
      str_wrap(short_names, width = 50)
    }) +
    scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0.1, 0.1)))  # Add padding to y-axis
  
  # Save the plot
  plot_filename <- paste0(filename_base, "_top20_barplot.png")
  plot_path <- file.path(main_results_dir, plot_filename)
  
  ggsave(plot_path, plot = barplot_humann, dpi = 300, 
         width = plot_width, height = plot_height, bg = "white")
  message(paste("Saved barplot to:", plot_path))
  
  # Also save as PDF for high quality
  pdf_filename <- paste0(filename_base, "_top20_barplot.pdf")
  pdf_path <- file.path(main_results_dir, pdf_filename)
  ggsave(pdf_path, plot = barplot_humann, width = plot_width, height = plot_height)
  
  return(barplot_humann)
}

# FUNCTION: Create individual analysis summary
create_analysis_summary <- function(variable, group_name, timepoint, 
                                   analysis_method, normalization, transformation,
                                   n_samples, n_features, n_total_results,
                                   n_sig_q_0_1, n_sig_q_0_05_coef_1,
                                   error_message = NA, output_dir = NA) {
  
  summary_df <- data.frame(
    Variable = variable,
    Group = group_name,
    Timepoint = timepoint,
    Analysis_Method = analysis_method,
    Normalization = normalization,
    Transformation = transformation,
    N_Samples = n_samples,
    N_Features = n_features,
    N_Total_Results = n_total_results,
    N_Sig_q_0_1 = n_sig_q_0_1,
    N_Sig_q_0_05_coef_1 = n_sig_q_0_05_coef_1,
    Success = ifelse(is.na(error_message), "YES", "NO"),
    Error_Message = ifelse(is.na(error_message), "", error_message),
    Output_Directory = ifelse(is.na(output_dir), "", output_dir),
    Timestamp = Sys.time(),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  
  return(summary_df)
}

# FUNCTION: Run MaAsLin2 with specified parameters
run_maaslin_analysis <- function(input_data, input_metadata, variable, group_name, 
                                 timepoint, analysis_method = "LM", 
                                 normalization = "TSS", transformation = "NONE") {
  
  # Create descriptive output directory name
  output_dir <- paste0("maaslin2_", variable, "_", group_name, "_", timepoint, 
                      "_", analysis_method, "_", normalization)
  
  # Create output directory in main results folder
  output_path <- file.path(main_results_dir, output_dir)
  
  # Convert to data frames (not tibbles)
  input_data_df <- as.data.frame(input_data)
  input_metadata_df <- as.data.frame(input_metadata)
  
  # Clean feature names (remove special characters)
  colnames(input_data_df) <- make.names(colnames(input_data_df))
  
  tryCatch({
    fit_data <- Maaslin2(
      input_data = input_data_df,
      input_metadata = input_metadata_df,
      output = output_path,  # Save in main results directory
      fixed_effects = variable,
      normalization = normalization,
      transform = transformation,
      analysis_method = analysis_method,
      correction = "BH",
      cores = 4,
      max_significance = 0.1,
      plot_heatmap = FALSE,
      plot_scatter = FALSE
    )
    
    return(fit_data)
    
  }, error = function(e) {
    error_msg <- paste("Error in", output_dir, ":", e$message)
    message(error_msg)
    return(list(error = error_msg, output_dir = output_dir))
  })
}

# FUNCTION: Extract and format results
extract_results <- function(fit_data, variable, group_name, timepoint, 
                            analysis_method, normalization, transformation) {
  if(is.null(fit_data) || !is.null(fit_data$error)) return(NULL)
  
  results <- fit_data$results
  if(nrow(results) == 0) return(NULL)
  
  results$Variable <- variable
  results$Group <- group_name
  results$Timepoint <- timepoint
  results$Analysis_Method <- analysis_method
  results$Normalization <- normalization
  results$Transformation <- transformation
  
  # Keep only significant results (q < 0.1)
  sig_results <- results %>% 
    filter(qval < 0.1) %>%
    dplyr::select(feature, Variable, Group, Timepoint, coef, stderr, pval, qval, 
                  Analysis_Method, Normalization, Transformation)
  
  return(sig_results)
}

# MAIN ANALYSIS - Analyze ALL variables
all_combined_results <- list()
all_barplots <- list()
all_analysis_summaries <- list()

# Define groups and timepoints
groups <- c("A", "B", "C")
timepoints <- c("-1", "-2")

# Transform data to compositional for LM and CPLM - EXACTLY like your original code
ps_obj_compositional <- microbiome::transform(ps_obj, 'compositional')

# Prepare input data for LM and CPLM - EXACTLY like your original code
input_data_compositional <- phyloseq::otu_table(ps_obj_compositional) %>%
  as.data.frame()
input_metadata_compositional <- data.frame(sample_data(ps_obj_compositional))

# Prepare input data for NEGBIN - EXACTLY like your original code
input_data_negbin <- phyloseq::otu_table(ps_obj) %>%
  as.data.frame()
input_metadata_negbin <- data.frame(sample_data(ps_obj))

# Define the combinations as in your original code
lm_normalizations <- c("NONE", "TSS")
lm_transformations <- c("NONE")

negbin_normalizations <- c('CSS', 'NONE', 'TMM')
negbin_transformations <- c("NONE")

# For each variable to analyze - ALL VARIABLES
for(variable in filtered_vars) {
  message(paste("\n" %+% paste(rep("=", 80), collapse = "")))
  message(paste("=== STARTING ANALYSIS FOR VARIABLE:", variable, "==="))
  message(paste(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""))
  
  variable_results <- list()
  variable_barplots <- list()
  n_analyses_for_variable <- 0
  n_successful_analyses_for_variable <- 0
  
  # Analysis for each Group × Timepoint combination
  for(group in groups) {
    for(tp in timepoints) {
      message(paste("\n" %+% paste(rep("-", 60), collapse = "")))
      message(paste("Analysis for:", variable, "- Group", group, "- Timepoint", tp))
      message(paste(rep("-", 60), collapse = ""))
      
      # Get indices for this group and timepoint
      comb_indices <- which(input_metadata_compositional$Group == group & 
                            input_metadata_compositional$Timepoint == tp)
      
      n_samples <- length(comb_indices)
      message(paste("Number of samples:", n_samples))
      
      if(n_samples >= 3) {
        # Subset data for this combination
        data_comb_comp <- input_data_compositional[, comb_indices, drop = FALSE]
        metadata_comb_comp <- input_metadata_compositional[comb_indices, , drop = FALSE]
        
        data_comb_raw <- input_data_negbin[, comb_indices, drop = FALSE]
        metadata_comb_raw <- input_metadata_negbin[comb_indices, , drop = FALSE]
        
        # Check variable in this subset
        var_values <- metadata_comb_comp[[variable]]
        var_type <- class(var_values)
        unique_vals <- unique(var_values[!is.na(var_values)])
        n_unique <- length(unique_vals)
        
        message(paste("Variable type in subset:", var_type))
        message(paste("Number of unique values:", n_unique))
        if(var_type == "factor" && n_unique <= 10) {
          message(paste("Levels:", paste(unique_vals, collapse = ", ")))
        }
        
        # Check number of features
        n_features_comp <- nrow(data_comb_comp)
        n_features_raw <- nrow(data_comb_raw)
        message(paste("Number of features (compositional):", n_features_comp))
        message(paste("Number of features (raw):", n_features_raw))
        
        # Run LM for this combination
        for(norm in lm_normalizations) {
          for(trans in lm_transformations) {
            n_analyses_for_variable <- n_analyses_for_variable + 1
            
            message(paste("\n  LM Analysis:", norm, "normalization,", trans, "transformation"))
            
            fit_comb_lm <- run_maaslin_analysis(
              input_data = data_comb_comp,
              input_metadata = metadata_comb_comp,
              variable = variable,
              group_name = group,
              timepoint = tp,
              analysis_method = "LM",
              normalization = norm,
              transformation = trans
            )
            
            # Create analysis summary
            if(!is.null(fit_comb_lm) && is.null(fit_comb_lm$error)) {
              n_successful_analyses_for_variable <- n_successful_analyses_for_variable + 1
              
              # Extract results for summary
              all_results <- fit_comb_lm$results
              n_total_results <- nrow(all_results)
              n_sig_q_0_1 <- sum(all_results$qval < 0.1, na.rm = TRUE)
              n_sig_q_0_05_coef_1 <- sum(all_results$qval <= 0.05 & abs(all_results$coef) >= 1, na.rm = TRUE)
              
              message(paste("    Total results:", n_total_results))
              message(paste("    Results with q < 0.1:", n_sig_q_0_1))
              message(paste("    Results with q ≤ 0.05 & |coef| ≥ 1:", n_sig_q_0_05_coef_1))
              
              # Create summary
              summary_df <- create_analysis_summary(
                variable = variable,
                group_name = group,
                timepoint = tp,
                analysis_method = "LM",
                normalization = norm,
                transformation = trans,
                n_samples = n_samples,
                n_features = n_features_comp,
                n_total_results = n_total_results,
                n_sig_q_0_1 = n_sig_q_0_1,
                n_sig_q_0_05_coef_1 = n_sig_q_0_05_coef_1,
                output_dir = paste0("maaslin2_", variable, "_", group, "_", tp, "_LM_", norm)
              )
              
              # Save individual summary
              individual_summary_file <- paste0(variable, "_", group, "_", tp, "_LM_", norm, "_summary.csv")
              write.csv(summary_df, file.path(main_results_dir, individual_summary_file), row.names = FALSE)
              
              all_analysis_summaries[[length(all_analysis_summaries) + 1]] <- summary_df
              
              # Extract significant results
              res_comb_lm <- extract_results(fit_comb_lm, variable,
                                             group_name = group,
                                             timepoint = tp,
                                             analysis_method = "LM",
                                             normalization = norm,
                                             transformation = trans)
              if(!is.null(res_comb_lm) && nrow(res_comb_lm) > 0) {
                variable_results[[length(variable_results) + 1]] <- res_comb_lm
                
                # Create barplot for this result
                barplot_comb_lm <- create_barplot(
                  res_comb_lm, 
                  variable = variable,
                  group_name = group,
                  timepoint = tp,
                  analysis_method = "LM",
                  normalization = norm,
                  transformation = trans
                )
                
                if(!is.null(barplot_comb_lm)) {
                  variable_barplots[[length(variable_barplots) + 1]] <- barplot_comb_lm
                  all_barplots[[length(all_barplots) + 1]] <- barplot_comb_lm
                }
              }
            } else if(!is.null(fit_comb_lm) && !is.null(fit_comb_lm$error)) {
              # Create error summary
              summary_df <- create_analysis_summary(
                variable = variable,
                group_name = group,
                timepoint = tp,
                analysis_method = "LM",
                normalization = norm,
                transformation = trans,
                n_samples = n_samples,
                n_features = n_features_comp,
                n_total_results = 0,
                n_sig_q_0_1 = 0,
                n_sig_q_0_05_coef_1 = 0,
                error_message = fit_comb_lm$error,
                output_dir = ifelse(!is.null(fit_comb_lm$output_dir), fit_comb_lm$output_dir, NA)
              )
              
              all_analysis_summaries[[length(all_analysis_summaries) + 1]] <- summary_df
            }
          }
        }
        
        # Run NEGBIN for this combination
        for(norm in negbin_normalizations) {
          for(trans in negbin_transformations) {
            n_analyses_for_variable <- n_analyses_for_variable + 1
            
            message(paste("\n  NEGBIN Analysis:", norm, "normalization,", trans, "transformation"))
            
            fit_comb_negbin <- run_maaslin_analysis(
              input_data = data_comb_raw,
              input_metadata = metadata_comb_raw,
              variable = variable,
              group_name = group,
              timepoint = tp,
              analysis_method = "NEGBIN",
              normalization = norm,
              transformation = trans
            )
            
            # Create analysis summary
            if(!is.null(fit_comb_negbin) && is.null(fit_comb_negbin$error)) {
              n_successful_analyses_for_variable <- n_successful_analyses_for_variable + 1
              
              # Extract results for summary
              all_results <- fit_comb_negbin$results
              n_total_results <- nrow(all_results)
              n_sig_q_0_1 <- sum(all_results$qval < 0.1, na.rm = TRUE)
              n_sig_q_0_05_coef_1 <- sum(all_results$qval <= 0.05 & abs(all_results$coef) >= 1, na.rm = TRUE)
              
              message(paste("    Total results:", n_total_results))
              message(paste("    Results with q < 0.1:", n_sig_q_0_1))
              message(paste("    Results with q ≤ 0.05 & |coef| ≥ 1:", n_sig_q_0_05_coef_1))
              
              # Create summary
              summary_df <- create_analysis_summary(
                variable = variable,
                group_name = group,
                timepoint = tp,
                analysis_method = "NEGBIN",
                normalization = norm,
                transformation = trans,
                n_samples = n_samples,
                n_features = n_features_raw,
                n_total_results = n_total_results,
                n_sig_q_0_1 = n_sig_q_0_1,
                n_sig_q_0_05_coef_1 = n_sig_q_0_05_coef_1,
                output_dir = paste0("maaslin2_", variable, "_", group, "_", tp, "_NEGBIN_", norm)
              )
              
              # Save individual summary
              individual_summary_file <- paste0(variable, "_", group, "_", tp, "_NEGBIN_", norm, "_summary.csv")
              write.csv(summary_df, file.path(main_results_dir, individual_summary_file), row.names = FALSE)
              
              all_analysis_summaries[[length(all_analysis_summaries) + 1]] <- summary_df
              
              # Extract significant results
              res_comb_negbin <- extract_results(fit_comb_negbin, variable,
                                                 group_name = group,
                                                 timepoint = tp,
                                                 analysis_method = "NEGBIN",
                                                 normalization = norm,
                                                 transformation = trans)
              if(!is.null(res_comb_negbin) && nrow(res_comb_negbin) > 0) {
                variable_results[[length(variable_results) + 1]] <- res_comb_negbin
                
                # Create barplot for this result
                barplot_comb_negbin <- create_barplot(
                  res_comb_negbin, 
                  variable = variable,
                  group_name = group,
                  timepoint = tp,
                  analysis_method = "NEGBIN",
                  normalization = norm,
                  transformation = trans
                )
                
                if(!is.null(barplot_comb_negbin)) {
                  variable_barplots[[length(variable_barplots) + 1]] <- barplot_comb_negbin
                  all_barplots[[length(all_barplots) + 1]] <- barplot_comb_negbin
                }
              }
            } else if(!is.null(fit_comb_negbin) && !is.null(fit_comb_negbin$error)) {
              # Create error summary
              summary_df <- create_analysis_summary(
                variable = variable,
                group_name = group,
                timepoint = tp,
                analysis_method = "NEGBIN",
                normalization = norm,
                transformation = trans,
                n_samples = n_samples,
                n_features = n_features_raw,
                n_total_results = 0,
                n_sig_q_0_1 = 0,
                n_sig_q_0_05_coef_1 = 0,
                error_message = fit_comb_negbin$error,
                output_dir = ifelse(!is.null(fit_comb_negbin$output_dir), fit_comb_negbin$output_dir, NA)
              )
              
              all_analysis_summaries[[length(all_analysis_summaries) + 1]] <- summary_df
            }
          }
        }
      } else {
        message(paste("\n  Skipping - insufficient samples (", n_samples, ")"))
        # Create summary for skipped analysis
        summary_df <- create_analysis_summary(
          variable = variable,
          group_name = group,
          timepoint = tp,
          analysis_method = "SKIPPED",
          normalization = "NONE",
          transformation = "NONE",
          n_samples = n_samples,
          n_features = 0,
          n_total_results = 0,
          n_sig_q_0_1 = 0,
          n_sig_q_0_05_coef_1 = 0,
          error_message = paste("Insufficient samples:", n_samples, "< 3")
        )
        
        all_analysis_summaries[[length(all_analysis_summaries) + 1]] <- summary_df
      }
    }
  }
  
  # Combine all results for this variable
  if(length(variable_results) > 0) {
    combined_variable_results <- bind_rows(variable_results)
    all_combined_results[[variable]] <- combined_variable_results
    message(paste("\n" %+% paste(rep("-", 60), collapse = "")))
    message(paste("SUMMARY FOR VARIABLE:", variable))
    message(paste("  - Total analyses attempted:", n_analyses_for_variable))
    message(paste("  - Successful analyses:", n_successful_analyses_for_variable))
    message(paste("  - Analyses with significant results:", length(variable_results)))
    message(paste("  - Barplots created:", length(variable_barplots)))
    message(paste("  - Total significant associations:", nrow(combined_variable_results)))
  } else {
    message(paste("\n" %+% paste(rep("-", 60), collapse = "")))
    message(paste("NO SIGNIFICANT RESULTS FOR VARIABLE:", variable))
    message(paste("  - Total analyses attempted:", n_analyses_for_variable))
    message(paste("  - Successful analyses:", n_successful_analyses_for_variable))
  }
}

# Combine all analysis summaries
if(length(all_analysis_summaries) > 0) {
  all_summaries_df <- bind_rows(all_analysis_summaries)
  
  # Save comprehensive summary
  write.csv(all_summaries_df, file.path(main_results_dir, "all_analysis_summaries.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
  message(paste("\nSaved all analysis summaries to:", file.path(main_results_dir, "all_analysis_summaries.csv")))
  
  # Create summary statistics
  summary_stats <- all_summaries_df %>%
    filter(Success == "YES") %>%
    group_by(Variable, Analysis_Method, Normalization) %>%
    summarise(
      N_Analyses = n(),
      Mean_Samples = mean(N_Samples, na.rm = TRUE),
      Mean_Features = mean(N_Features, na.rm = TRUE),
      Total_Sig_q_0_1 = sum(N_Sig_q_0_1, na.rm = TRUE),
      Total_Sig_q_0_05_coef_1 = sum(N_Sig_q_0_05_coef_1, na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = 'drop'
    )
  
  write.csv(summary_stats, file.path(main_results_dir, "analysis_summary_statistics.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
  message(paste("Saved analysis summary statistics to:", file.path(main_results_dir, "analysis_summary_statistics.csv")))
  
  # Print overall summary
  message("\n=== OVERALL ANALYSIS SUMMARY ===")
  print(summary_stats)
}

# Save all results to main directory
if(length(all_combined_results) > 0) {
  # Save combined results
  all_results_df <- bind_rows(all_combined_results)
  write.csv(all_results_df, file.path(main_results_dir, "maaslin2_all_results.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
  message(paste("\nSaved all results to:", file.path(main_results_dir, "maaslin2_all_results.csv")))
  
  # Create summary statistics
  summary_stats <- all_results_df %>%
    group_by(Variable, Group, Timepoint, Analysis_Method, Normalization) %>%
    summarise(
      n_significant = n(),
      mean_coef = mean(coef),
      min_qval = min(qval),
      .groups = 'drop'
    )
  
  write.csv(summary_stats, file.path(main_results_dir, "maaslin2_summary_statistics.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
  message(paste("Saved summary statistics to:", file.path(main_results_dir, "maaslin2_summary_statistics.csv")))
} else {
  message("\nNo significant results found for any variable.")
}

# Print completion message
message("\n" %+% paste(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""))
message("=== ANALYSIS COMPLETED ===")
message(paste(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""))
message(paste("All results saved in:", main_results_dir))
message("\nGenerated files:")
message("1. Individual MaAsLin2 output directories for each analysis")
message("2. Individual analysis summary CSV files for each analysis")
message("3. Combined analysis summaries: all_analysis_summaries.csv")
message("4. Analysis summary statistics: analysis_summary_statistics.csv")
if(length(all_combined_results) > 0) {
  message("5. Top-20 barplots for each significant result (as PNG/PDF files)")
  message("6. Filtered CSV files with top-20 features")
  message("7. Combined results: maaslin2_all_results.csv")
  message("8. Summary statistics: maaslin2_summary_statistics.csv")
}
message(paste("\nTotal analyses attempted:", nrow(all_summaries_df)))
message(paste("Successful analyses:", sum(all_summaries_df$Success == "YES", na.rm = TRUE)))
message(paste("Failed analyses:", sum(all_summaries_df$Success == "NO", na.rm = TRUE)))
message(paste("Skipped analyses:", sum(all_summaries_df$Analysis_Method == "SKIPPED", na.rm = TRUE)))
```

# upd jan 20: no duplicates
```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(Maaslin2)
library(microbiome)
library(phyloseq)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)
library(ggpubr)
library(stringr)

# Create main results directory
main_results_dir <- "/home/sonec_iv/RSF_humann_res"
if (!dir.exists(main_results_dir)) {
  dir.create(main_results_dir, recursive = TRUE)
}
setwd(main_results_dir)

# Load your phyloseq object
ps_hm <- readRDS('/home/sonec_iv/RSF_res_4_ps/hm3/RSF_ps_humann_joint.rds')
ps_obj <- ps_hm

# Check and prepare the data - EXACTLY like your original code
otu_matrix <- otu_table(ps_obj)

if (is.matrix(otu_matrix)) {
  # Round all numeric values
  otu_matrix[] <- round(otu_matrix)
  # Assign the rounded matrix back to the otu_table
  otu_table(ps_obj) <- otu_table(otu_matrix, taxa_are_rows = taxa_are_rows(otu_table(ps_obj)))
} else {
  stop("otu_table is not a matrix.")
}

# Apply prevalence filter
prevalence <- 10/100
detection <- 1/100
taxas <- core_members(ps_obj, detection = detection, prevalence = prevalence, include.lowest = F)
ps_obj <- prune_taxa(taxas, ps_obj)

# Prepare metadata - FIXED: Use data.frame() not as.data.frame()
metadf <- data.frame(sample_data(ps_obj))

# Fix column types
numeric_cols <- c("Age", "Weight", "Height", "BMI")
for(col in numeric_cols) {
  if(col %in% names(metadf)) {
    metadf[[col]] <- as.numeric(metadf[[col]])
  }
}

# Define variables to analyze
excluded_columns <- c("Batch", "No_Patient", "Group", "SampleID", "Timepoint", 
                      "Patient_ID", "Sample_collection_date", "Sample", "ID", "X")

# Get all potential variables
all_vars <- names(metadf)[!names(metadf) %in% excluded_columns]
#all_vars <- "Antihypertensive_Drugs"
# Filter variables that have at least 2 unique values
filtered_vars <- character(0)
for(var in all_vars) {
  unique_vals <- unique(metadf[[var]])
  unique_vals <- unique_vals[!is.na(unique_vals)]
  if(length(unique_vals) >= 2 && length(unique_vals) < nrow(metadf) * 0.9) {
    filtered_vars <- c(filtered_vars, var)
  }
}

message(paste("\nFound", length(filtered_vars), "variables to analyze:"))
message(paste(filtered_vars, collapse = ", "))

# Convert filtered variables to factors and print variable info
message("\n=== VARIABLE INFORMATION ===")
for(col in filtered_vars) {
  if(col %in% names(metadf)) {
    metadf[[col]] <- as.factor(metadf[[col]])
    # Get variable information
    unique_vals <- unique(metadf[[col]])
    unique_vals <- unique_vals[!is.na(unique_vals)]
    n_levels <- length(unique_vals)
    var_type <- class(metadf[[col]])
    
    message(paste("Variable:", col))
    message(paste("  Type:", var_type))
    message(paste("  Number of levels/unique values:", n_levels))
    if(var_type == "factor" && n_levels <= 10) {
      message(paste("  Levels:", paste(unique_vals, collapse = ", ")))
    } else if(var_type == "numeric") {
      message(paste("  Range:", min(metadf[[col]], na.rm = TRUE), "to", max(metadf[[col]], na.rm = TRUE)))
      message(paste("  Mean:", mean(metadf[[col]], na.rm = TRUE)))
    }
    message(paste("  NAs:", sum(is.na(metadf[[col]]))))
    message("")
  }
}

# Update sample data in phyloseq object
sample_data(ps_obj) <- sample_data(metadf)

# FUNCTION: Create barplot for significant results
create_barplot <- function(results_df, variable, group_name, timepoint, 
                          analysis_method, normalization, transformation) {
  
  # Filter significant results (qval <= 0.05 and |coef| >= 1)
  filtered_results <- results_df %>%
    filter(qval <= 0.05 & abs(coef) >= 1)
  
  if(nrow(filtered_results) == 0) {
    message(paste("No significant results for", variable, "Group", group_name, 
                  "Timepoint", timepoint, "with current thresholds"))
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # Select top 20 features by absolute coefficient value
  filtered_results <- filtered_results %>%
    mutate(abs_coef = abs(coef)) %>%
    arrange(desc(abs_coef)) %>%
    head(20)
  
  # Check for duplicate feature names
  duplicate_features <- filtered_results$feature[duplicated(filtered_results$feature)]
  if(length(duplicate_features) > 0) {
    message(paste("WARNING: Found", length(duplicate_features), "duplicate feature names:"))
    message(paste(duplicate_features, collapse = ", "))
    
    # Remove duplicates, keeping the one with highest absolute coefficient
    filtered_results <- filtered_results %>%
      group_by(feature) %>%
      arrange(desc(abs_coef)) %>%
      slice(1) %>%
      ungroup()
    
    message(paste("Removed duplicates, now have", nrow(filtered_results), "unique features"))
  }
  
  # Add group column for coloring
  filtered_results <- filtered_results %>%
    mutate(group = case_when(
      coef > 0 ~ "y", 
      coef < 0 ~ "n"
    ))
  
  # Sort by coefficient for plotting
  filtered_results <- filtered_results %>%
    arrange(coef)
  
  # Create filename based on parameters
  filename_base <- paste0(variable, "_", group_name, "_", timepoint, "_", 
                         analysis_method, "_", normalization)
  
  # Save filtered results to CSV
  csv_filename <- paste0(filename_base, "_top20_filtered.csv")
  write.csv(filtered_results, file.path(main_results_dir, csv_filename), row.names = FALSE)
  
  # Create barplot
  size <- 10  # Reduced font size for better fit with full feature names
  
  # Create title based on parameters
  plot_title <- paste0(variable, " (Group ", group_name, ", Timepoint ", timepoint, 
                      ")\n", analysis_method, " with ", normalization, " normalization")
  
  # Calculate dynamic dimensions based on feature name lengths
  n_features <- nrow(filtered_results)
  
  # Calculate maximum feature name length to determine plot width
  max_feature_length <- max(nchar(as.character(filtered_results$feature)), na.rm = TRUE)
  
  # Adjust plot width based on feature name length
  # Base width: 12 inches, add 0.05 inches per character in longest name
  plot_width <- max(12, 12 + (max_feature_length * 0.05))
  plot_height <- max(8, min(15, n_features * 0.4))  # Increased height for more space
  
  # Create factor with levels in order of coefficient for proper bar ordering
  # Use the original feature names (which should be unique from the otu_table)
  filtered_results$feature <- factor(filtered_results$feature, 
                                     levels = filtered_results$feature)
  
  # Create the plot with ggplot directly for better control
  barplot_humann <- ggplot(filtered_results, aes(x = feature, y = coef, fill = group)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 0.6, position = position_dodge(0.8)) +  # Increased bar spacing
    scale_fill_manual(values = c('n' = "#1BA3C6", 'y' = "#D81B60")) +
    coord_flip() +  # Flip for horizontal bars with y-axis labels on left
    labs(
      title = plot_title,
      x = "Feature",
      y = "Coefficient"
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      axis.text.y = element_text(color = "black", size = size, hjust = 1, 
                                 margin = margin(r = 10)),
      axis.text.x = element_text(color = "black", size = size),  # Added x-axis text
      axis.title.y = element_text(color = "black", size = size + 2, margin = margin(r = 15)),
      axis.title.x = element_text(color = "black", size = size + 2, margin = margin(t = 10)),
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = size + 2, face = "bold", margin = margin(b = 15)),
      legend.position = "none",
      panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey90", linewidth = 0.2),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      plot.margin = margin(20, 20, 20, 40)  # Increased left margin for long feature names
    ) +
    scale_x_discrete(labels = function(x) str_wrap(x, width = 80)) +  # Wrap very long names
    scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0.1, 0.1)))  # Add padding to y-axis
  
  # Save the plot
  plot_filename <- paste0(filename_base, "_top20_barplot.png")
  plot_path <- file.path(main_results_dir, plot_filename)
  
  ggsave(plot_path, plot = barplot_humann, dpi = 300, 
         width = plot_width, height = plot_height, bg = "white", limitsize = FALSE)
  message(paste("Saved barplot to:", plot_path))
  message(paste("Plot dimensions:", round(plot_width, 1), "x", round(plot_height, 1), "inches"))
  
  # Also save as PDF for high quality
  pdf_filename <- paste0(filename_base, "_top20_barplot.pdf")
  pdf_path <- file.path(main_results_dir, pdf_filename)
  ggsave(pdf_path, plot = barplot_humann, width = plot_width, height = plot_height, limitsize = FALSE)
  
  return(barplot_humann)
}

# FUNCTION: Create individual analysis summary
create_analysis_summary <- function(variable, group_name, timepoint, 
                                   analysis_method, normalization, transformation,
                                   n_samples, n_features, n_total_results,
                                   n_sig_q_0_1, n_sig_q_0_05_coef_1,
                                   error_message = NA, output_dir = NA) {
  
  summary_df <- data.frame(
    Variable = variable,
    Group = group_name,
    Timepoint = timepoint,
    Analysis_Method = analysis_method,
    Normalization = normalization,
    Transformation = transformation,
    N_Samples = n_samples,
    N_Features = n_features,
    N_Total_Results = n_total_results,
    N_Sig_q_0_1 = n_sig_q_0_1,
    N_Sig_q_0_05_coef_1 = n_sig_q_0_05_coef_1,
    Success = ifelse(is.na(error_message), "YES", "NO"),
    Error_Message = ifelse(is.na(error_message), "", error_message),
    Output_Directory = ifelse(is.na(output_dir), "", output_dir),
    Timestamp = Sys.time(),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  
  return(summary_df)
}

# FUNCTION: Run MaAsLin2 with specified parameters
run_maaslin_analysis <- function(input_data, input_metadata, variable, group_name, 
                                 timepoint, analysis_method = "LM", 
                                 normalization = "TSS", transformation = "NONE") {
  
  # Create descriptive output directory name
  output_dir <- paste0("maaslin2_", variable, "_", group_name, "_", timepoint, 
                      "_", analysis_method, "_", normalization)
  
  # Create output directory in main results folder
  output_path <- file.path(main_results_dir, output_dir)
  
  # Convert to data frames (not tibbles)
  input_data_df <- as.data.frame(input_data)
  input_metadata_df <- as.data.frame(input_metadata)
  
  # IMPORTANT: MaAsLin2 expects features as COLUMNS and samples as ROWS
  # So we need to transpose our data
  input_data_df <- as.data.frame(t(input_data_df))
  
  # Clean feature names (remove special characters) - features are now column names
  original_names <- colnames(input_data_df)
  clean_names <- make.names(original_names)
  colnames(input_data_df) <- clean_names
  
  # Store mapping for reference
  name_mapping <- data.frame(
    original = original_names,
    clean = clean_names,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  
  tryCatch({
    fit_data <- Maaslin2(
      input_data = input_data_df,
      input_metadata = input_metadata_df,
      output = output_path,  # Save in main results directory
      fixed_effects = variable,
      normalization = normalization,
      transform = transformation,
      analysis_method = analysis_method,
      correction = "BH",
      cores = 4,
      max_significance = 0.1,
      plot_heatmap = FALSE,
      plot_scatter = FALSE
    )
    
    # Restore original feature names in results
    if (!is.null(fit_data$results)) {
      # Map clean names back to original names
      fit_data$results$feature <- name_mapping$original[match(fit_data$results$feature, name_mapping$clean)]
    }
    
    return(fit_data)
    
  }, error = function(e) {
    error_msg <- paste("Error in", output_dir, ":", e$message)
    message(error_msg)
    return(list(error = error_msg, output_dir = output_dir))
  })
}

# FUNCTION: Extract and format results
extract_results <- function(fit_data, variable, group_name, timepoint, 
                            analysis_method, normalization, transformation) {
  if(is.null(fit_data) || !is.null(fit_data$error)) return(NULL)
  
  results <- fit_data$results
  if(is.null(results) || nrow(results) == 0) return(NULL)
  
  results$Variable <- variable
  results$Group <- group_name
  results$Timepoint <- timepoint
  results$Analysis_Method <- analysis_method
  results$Normalization <- normalization
  results$Transformation <- transformation
  
  # Check for duplicate feature names in results
  duplicate_features <- results$feature[duplicated(results$feature)]
  if(length(duplicate_features) > 0) {
    message(paste("WARNING: MaAsLin2 returned", length(duplicate_features), 
                  "duplicate feature names for", variable, group_name, timepoint))
    message(paste("Sample duplicates:", paste(head(duplicate_features, 5), collapse = ", ")))
    
    # Remove duplicates, keeping the one with lowest q-value
    results <- results %>%
      group_by(feature) %>%
      arrange(qval) %>%
      slice(1) %>%
      ungroup()
  }
  
  # Keep only significant results (q < 0.1)
  sig_results <- results %>% 
    filter(qval < 0.1) %>%
    dplyr::select(feature, Variable, Group, Timepoint, coef, stderr, pval, qval, 
                  Analysis_Method, Normalization, Transformation)
  
  return(sig_results)
}

# MAIN ANALYSIS - Analyze ALL variables
all_combined_results <- list()
all_barplots <- list()
all_analysis_summaries <- list()

# Define groups and timepoints
groups <- c("A", "B", "C")
timepoints <- c("-1", "-2")

# Transform data to compositional for LM and CPLM - EXACTLY like your original code
ps_obj_compositional <- microbiome::transform(ps_obj, 'compositional')

# Prepare input data for LM and CPLM - EXACTLY like your original code
input_data_compositional <- phyloseq::otu_table(ps_obj_compositional) %>%
  as.data.frame()
input_metadata_compositional <- data.frame(sample_data(ps_obj_compositional))

# Prepare input data for NEGBIN - EXACTLY like your original code
input_data_negbin <- phyloseq::otu_table(ps_obj) %>%
  as.data.frame()
input_metadata_negbin <- data.frame(sample_data(ps_obj))

# Check for duplicate feature names in the original data
duplicate_features_comp <- rownames(input_data_compositional)[duplicated(rownames(input_data_compositional))]
duplicate_features_raw <- rownames(input_data_negbin)[duplicated(rownames(input_data_negbin))]

if(length(duplicate_features_comp) > 0) {
  message(paste("WARNING: Found", length(duplicate_features_comp), 
                "duplicate feature names in compositional data"))
  message(paste("First 5 duplicates:", paste(head(duplicate_features_comp, 5), collapse = ", ")))
}

if(length(duplicate_features_raw) > 0) {
  message(paste("WARNING: Found", length(duplicate_features_raw), 
                "duplicate feature names in raw data"))
  message(paste("First 5 duplicates:", paste(head(duplicate_features_raw, 5), collapse = ", ")))
}

# Define the combinations as in your original code
lm_normalizations <- c("NONE", "TSS")
lm_transformations <- c("NONE")

negbin_normalizations <- c('CSS', 'NONE', 'TMM')
negbin_transformations <- c("NONE")

# For each variable to analyze - ALL VARIABLES
for(variable in filtered_vars) {
  message(paste("\n", paste(rep("=", 80), collapse = "")))
  message(paste("=== STARTING ANALYSIS FOR VARIABLE:", variable, "==="))
  message(paste(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""))
  
  variable_results <- list()
  variable_barplots <- list()
  n_analyses_for_variable <- 0
  n_successful_analyses_for_variable <- 0
  
  # Analysis for each Group × Timepoint combination
  for(group in groups) {
    for(tp in timepoints) {
      message(paste("\n", paste(rep("-", 60), collapse = "")))
      message(paste("Analysis for:", variable, "- Group", group, "- Timepoint", tp))
      message(paste(rep("-", 60), collapse = ""))
      
      # Get indices for this group and timepoint
      comb_indices <- which(input_metadata_compositional$Group == group & 
                            input_metadata_compositional$Timepoint == tp)
      
      n_samples <- length(comb_indices)
      message(paste("Number of samples:", n_samples))
      
      if(n_samples >= 3) {
        # Subset data for this combination
        data_comb_comp <- input_data_compositional[, comb_indices, drop = FALSE]
        metadata_comb_comp <- input_metadata_compositional[comb_indices, , drop = FALSE]
        
        data_comb_raw <- input_data_negbin[, comb_indices, drop = FALSE]
        metadata_comb_raw <- input_metadata_negbin[comb_indices, , drop = FALSE]
        
        # Remove rows with all zeros
        data_comb_comp <- data_comb_comp[rowSums(data_comb_comp) > 0, , drop = FALSE]
        data_comb_raw <- data_comb_raw[rowSums(data_comb_raw) > 0, , drop = FALSE]
        
        # Check variable in this subset
        var_values <- metadata_comb_comp[[variable]]
        var_type <- class(var_values)
        unique_vals <- unique(var_values[!is.na(var_values)])
        n_unique <- length(unique_vals)
        
        message(paste("Variable type in subset:", var_type))
        message(paste("Number of unique values:", n_unique))
        if(var_type == "factor" && n_unique <= 10) {
          message(paste("Levels:", paste(unique_vals, collapse = ", ")))
        }
        
        # Check number of features
        n_features_comp <- nrow(data_comb_comp)
        n_features_raw <- nrow(data_comb_raw)
        message(paste("Number of features (compositional):", n_features_comp))
        message(paste("Number of features (raw):", n_features_raw))
        
        # Run LM for this combination
        for(norm in lm_normalizations) {
          for(trans in lm_transformations) {
            n_analyses_for_variable <- n_analyses_for_variable + 1
            
            message(paste("\n  LM Analysis:", norm, "normalization,", trans, "transformation"))
            
            fit_comb_lm <- run_maaslin_analysis(
              input_data = data_comb_comp,
              input_metadata = metadata_comb_comp,
              variable = variable,
              group_name = group,
              timepoint = tp,
              analysis_method = "LM",
              normalization = norm,
              transformation = trans
            )
            
            # Create analysis summary
            if(!is.null(fit_comb_lm) && is.null(fit_comb_lm$error)) {
              n_successful_analyses_for_variable <- n_successful_analyses_for_variable + 1
              
              # Extract results for summary
              all_results <- fit_comb_lm$results
              n_total_results <- ifelse(is.null(all_results), 0, nrow(all_results))
              n_sig_q_0_1 <- ifelse(is.null(all_results), 0, sum(all_results$qval < 0.1, na.rm = TRUE))
              n_sig_q_0_05_coef_1 <- ifelse(is.null(all_results), 0, 
                                           sum(all_results$qval <= 0.05 & abs(all_results$coef) >= 1, na.rm = TRUE))
              
              message(paste("    Total results:", n_total_results))
              message(paste("    Results with q < 0.1:", n_sig_q_0_1))
              message(paste("    Results with q ≤ 0.05 & |coef| ≥ 1:", n_sig_q_0_05_coef_1))
              
              # Create summary
              summary_df <- create_analysis_summary(
                variable = variable,
                group_name = group,
                timepoint = tp,
                analysis_method = "LM",
                normalization = norm,
                transformation = trans,
                n_samples = n_samples,
                n_features = n_features_comp,
                n_total_results = n_total_results,
                n_sig_q_0_1 = n_sig_q_0_1,
                n_sig_q_0_05_coef_1 = n_sig_q_0_05_coef_1,
                output_dir = paste0("maaslin2_", variable, "_", group, "_", tp, "_LM_", norm)
              )
              
              # Save individual summary
              individual_summary_file <- paste0(variable, "_", group, "_", tp, "_LM_", norm, "_summary.csv")
              write.csv(summary_df, file.path(main_results_dir, individual_summary_file), row.names = FALSE)
              
              all_analysis_summaries[[length(all_analysis_summaries) + 1]] <- summary_df
              
              # Extract significant results
              res_comb_lm <- extract_results(fit_comb_lm, variable,
                                             group_name = group,
                                             timepoint = tp,
                                             analysis_method = "LM",
                                             normalization = norm,
                                             transformation = trans)
              if(!is.null(res_comb_lm) && nrow(res_comb_lm) > 0) {
                variable_results[[length(variable_results) + 1]] <- res_comb_lm
                
                # Create barplot for this result
                barplot_comb_lm <- create_barplot(
                  res_comb_lm, 
                  variable = variable,
                  group_name = group,
                  timepoint = tp,
                  analysis_method = "LM",
                  normalization = norm,
                  transformation = trans
                )
                
                if(!is.null(barplot_comb_lm)) {
                  variable_barplots[[length(variable_barplots) + 1]] <- barplot_comb_lm
                  all_barplots[[length(all_barplots) + 1]] <- barplot_comb_lm
                }
              }
            } else if(!is.null(fit_comb_lm) && !is.null(fit_comb_lm$error)) {
              # Create error summary
              summary_df <- create_analysis_summary(
                variable = variable,
                group_name = group,
                timepoint = tp,
                analysis_method = "LM",
                normalization = norm,
                transformation = trans,
                n_samples = n_samples,
                n_features = n_features_comp,
                n_total_results = 0,
                n_sig_q_0_1 = 0,
```
