```{r}
                n_sig_q_0_05_coef_1 = 0,
                error_message = fit_comb_lm$error,
                output_dir = ifelse(!is.null(fit_comb_lm$output_dir), fit_comb_lm$output_dir, NA)
              )
              
              all_analysis_summaries[[length(all_analysis_summaries) + 1]] <- summary_df
            }
          }
        }
        
        # Run NEGBIN for this combination
        for(norm in negbin_normalizations) {
          for(trans in negbin_transformations) {
            n_analyses_for_variable <- n_analyses_for_variable + 1
            
            message(paste("\n  NEGBIN Analysis:", norm, "normalization,", trans, "transformation"))
            
            fit_comb_negbin <- run_maaslin_analysis(
              input_data = data_comb_raw,
              input_metadata = metadata_comb_raw,
              variable = variable,
              group_name = group,
              timepoint = tp,
              analysis_method = "NEGBIN",
              normalization = norm,
              transformation = trans
            )
            
            # Create analysis summary
            if(!is.null(fit_comb_negbin) && is.null(fit_comb_negbin$error)) {
              n_successful_analyses_for_variable <- n_successful_analyses_for_variable + 1
              
              # Extract results for summary
              all_results <- fit_comb_negbin$results
              n_total_results <- ifelse(is.null(all_results), 0, nrow(all_results))
              n_sig_q_0_1 <- ifelse(is.null(all_results), 0, sum(all_results$qval < 0.1, na.rm = TRUE))
              n_sig_q_0_05_coef_1 <- ifelse(is.null(all_results), 0, 
                                           sum(all_results$qval <= 0.05 & abs(all_results$coef) >= 1, na.rm = TRUE))
              
              message(paste("    Total results:", n_total_results))
              message(paste("    Results with q < 0.1:", n_sig_q_0_1))
              message(paste("    Results with q ≤ 0.05 & |coef| ≥ 1:", n_sig_q_0_05_coef_1))
              
              # Create summary
              summary_df <- create_analysis_summary(
                variable = variable,
                group_name = group,
                timepoint = tp,
                analysis_method = "NEGBIN",
                normalization = norm,
                transformation = trans,
                n_samples = n_samples,
                n_features = n_features_raw,
                n_total_results = n_total_results,
                n_sig_q_0_1 = n_sig_q_0_1,
                n_sig_q_0_05_coef_1 = n_sig_q_0_05_coef_1,
                output_dir = paste0("maaslin2_", variable, "_", group, "_", tp, "_NEGBIN_", norm)
              )
              
              # Save individual summary
              individual_summary_file <- paste0(variable, "_", group, "_", tp, "_NEGBIN_", norm, "_summary.csv")
              write.csv(summary_df, file.path(main_results_dir, individual_summary_file), row.names = FALSE)
              
              all_analysis_summaries[[length(all_analysis_summaries) + 1]] <- summary_df
              
              # Extract significant results
              res_comb_negbin <- extract_results(fit_comb_negbin, variable,
                                                 group_name = group,
                                                 timepoint = tp,
                                                 analysis_method = "NEGBIN",
                                                 normalization = norm,
                                                 transformation = trans)
              if(!is.null(res_comb_negbin) && nrow(res_comb_negbin) > 0) {
                variable_results[[length(variable_results) + 1]] <- res_comb_negbin
                
                # Create barplot for this result
                barplot_comb_negbin <- create_barplot(
                  res_comb_negbin, 
                  variable = variable,
                  group_name = group,
                  timepoint = tp,
                  analysis_method = "NEGBIN",
                  normalization = norm,
                  transformation = trans
                )
                
                if(!is.null(barplot_comb_negbin)) {
                  variable_barplots[[length(variable_barplots) + 1]] <- barplot_comb_negbin
                  all_barplots[[length(all_barplots) + 1]] <- barplot_comb_negbin
                }
              }
            } else if(!is.null(fit_comb_negbin) && !is.null(fit_comb_negbin$error)) {
              # Create error summary
              summary_df <- create_analysis_summary(
                variable = variable,
                group_name = group,
                timepoint = tp,
                analysis_method = "NEGBIN",
                normalization = norm,
                transformation = trans,
                n_samples = n_samples,
                n_features = n_features_raw,
                n_total_results = 0,
                n_sig_q_0_1 = 0,
                n_sig_q_0_05_coef_1 = 0,
                error_message = fit_comb_negbin$error,
                output_dir = ifelse(!is.null(fit_comb_negbin$output_dir), fit_comb_negbin$output_dir, NA)
              )
              
              all_analysis_summaries[[length(all_analysis_summaries) + 1]] <- summary_df
            }
          }
        }
      } else {
        message(paste("\n  Skipping - insufficient samples (", n_samples, ")"))
        # Create summary for skipped analysis
        summary_df <- create_analysis_summary(
          variable = variable,
          group_name = group,
          timepoint = tp,
          analysis_method = "SKIPPED",
          normalization = "NONE",
          transformation = "NONE",
          n_samples = n_samples,
          n_features = 0,
          n_total_results = 0,
          n_sig_q_0_1 = 0,
          n_sig_q_0_05_coef_1 = 0,
          error_message = paste("Insufficient samples:", n_samples, "< 3")
        )
        
        all_analysis_summaries[[length(all_analysis_summaries) + 1]] <- summary_df
      }
    }
  }
  
  # Combine all results for this variable
  if(length(variable_results) > 0) {
    combined_variable_results <- bind_rows(variable_results)
    all_combined_results[[variable]] <- combined_variable_results
    message(paste("\n", paste(rep("-", 60), collapse = "")))
    message(paste("SUMMARY FOR VARIABLE:", variable))
    message(paste("  - Total analyses attempted:", n_analyses_for_variable))
    message(paste("  - Successful analyses:", n_successful_analyses_for_variable))
    message(paste("  - Analyses with significant results:", length(variable_results)))
    message(paste("  - Barplots created:", length(variable_barplots)))
    message(paste("  - Total significant associations:", nrow(combined_variable_results)))
  } else {
    message(paste("\n", paste(rep("-", 60), collapse = "")))
    message(paste("NO SIGNIFICANT RESULTS FOR VARIABLE:", variable))
    message(paste("  - Total analyses attempted:", n_analyses_for_variable))
    message(paste("  - Successful analyses:", n_successful_analyses_for_variable))
  }
}

# Combine all analysis summaries
if(length(all_analysis_summaries) > 0) {
  all_summaries_df <- bind_rows(all_analysis_summaries)
  
  # Save comprehensive summary
  write.csv(all_summaries_df, file.path(main_results_dir, "all_analysis_summaries.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
  message(paste("\nSaved all analysis summaries to:", file.path(main_results_dir, "all_analysis_summaries.csv")))
  
  # Create summary statistics
  summary_stats <- all_summaries_df %>%
    filter(Success == "YES") %>%
    group_by(Variable, Analysis_Method, Normalization) %>%
    summarise(
      N_Analyses = n(),
      Mean_Samples = mean(N_Samples, na.rm = TRUE),
      Mean_Features = mean(N_Features, na.rm = TRUE),
      Total_Sig_q_0_1 = sum(N_Sig_q_0_1, na.rm = TRUE),
      Total_Sig_q_0_05_coef_1 = sum(N_Sig_q_0_05_coef_1, na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = 'drop'
    )
  
  write.csv(summary_stats, file.path(main_results_dir, "analysis_summary_statistics.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
  message(paste("Saved analysis summary statistics to:", file.path(main_results_dir, "analysis_summary_statistics.csv")))
  
  # Print overall summary
  message("\n=== OVERALL ANALYSIS SUMMARY ===")
  print(summary_stats)
}

# Save all results to main directory
if(length(all_combined_results) > 0) {
  # Save combined results
  all_results_df <- bind_rows(all_combined_results)
  write.csv(all_results_df, file.path(main_results_dir, "maaslin2_all_results.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
  message(paste("\nSaved all results to:", file.path(main_results_dir, "maaslin2_all_results.csv")))
  
  # Create summary statistics
  summary_stats <- all_results_df %>%
    group_by(Variable, Group, Timepoint, Analysis_Method, Normalization) %>%
    summarise(
      n_significant = n(),
      mean_coef = mean(coef),
      min_qval = min(qval),
      .groups = 'drop'
    )
  
  write.csv(summary_stats, file.path(main_results_dir, "maaslin2_summary_statistics.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
  message(paste("Saved summary statistics to:", file.path(main_results_dir, "maaslin2_summary_statistics.csv")))
  
  # Create simple summary plot
  if(nrow(summary_stats) > 0) {
    overall_plot <- ggplot(summary_stats, aes(x = paste(Group, Timepoint), y = n_significant, fill = Analysis_Method)) +
      geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge()) +
      facet_grid(Normalization ~ Variable, scales = "free") +
      labs(
        title = "Summary of Significant Associations",
        x = "Group × Timepoint",
        y = "Number of Significant Features (q < 0.1)",
        fill = "Analysis Method"
      ) +
      theme_minimal() +
      theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
    
    ggsave(file.path(main_results_dir, "maaslin2_overall_summary.pdf"), overall_plot, 
           width = max(16, length(unique(summary_stats$Variable)) * 3), 
           height = 10, limitsize = FALSE)
    ggsave(file.path(main_results_dir, "maaslin2_overall_summary.png"), overall_plot, 
           width = max(16, length(unique(summary_stats$Variable)) * 3), 
           height = 10, dpi = 300, limitsize = FALSE)
    message(paste("Saved overall summary plot to:", main_results_dir))
  }
} else {
  message("\nNo significant results found for any variable.")
}

# Print completion message
message(paste("\n", paste(rep("=", 80), collapse = "")))
message("=== ANALYSIS COMPLETED ===")
message(paste(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""))
message(paste("All results saved in:", main_results_dir))
message("\nGenerated files:")
message("1. Individual MaAsLin2 output directories for each analysis")
message("2. Individual analysis summary CSV files for each analysis")
message("3. Combined analysis summaries: all_analysis_summaries.csv")
message("4. Analysis summary statistics: analysis_summary_statistics.csv")
if(length(all_combined_results) > 0) {
  message("5. Top-20 barplots for each significant result (as PNG/PDF files)")
  message("6. Filtered CSV files with top-20 features")
  message("7. Combined results: maaslin2_all_results.csv")
  message("8. Summary statistics: maaslin2_summary_statistics.csv")
  message("9. Overall summary plot (maaslin2_overall_summary.pdf/png)")
}
message(paste("\nTotal analyses attempted:", nrow(all_summaries_df)))
message(paste("Successful analyses:", sum(all_summaries_df$Success == "YES", na.rm = TRUE)))
message(paste("Failed analyses:", sum(all_summaries_df$Success == "NO", na.rm = TRUE)))
message(paste("Skipped analyses:", sum(all_summaries_df$Analysis_Method == "SKIPPED", na.rm = TRUE)))
```

# upd mar 26: deseq2 like plots
```{r}
# ============================================================================
# COMBINED TIMEPOINT COMPARISON PLOTS (MaAsLin2) - All Groups Together
# ============================================================================
# This script analyzes HUMAnN data with MaAsLin2 to compare timepoints (-2 vs -1)
# within each variable level and group, and creates combined barplots showing
# significant features for all groups in the same plot.
# Features:
# - LM model: NONE/TSS normalization + CLR transformation (compositional data)
# - NEGBIN model: CSS/TMM normalization (raw counts)
# - Top 20 positive and top 20 negative coefficients (up to 40 total)
# - Ordered: most positive at top, most negative at bottom
# - Legend: "Enriched in -2" (positive coefficient, regular colors) and "Decreased in -2" (negative coefficient, light colors)
# ============================================================================

# Load required libraries
library(phyloseq)
library(microbiome)
library(Maaslin2)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)
library(stringr)

# ============================================================================
# SECTION 1: SETUP
# ============================================================================

cat("Starting combined timepoint analysis for HUMAnN data...\n")

# Create output directory
output_dir <- "combined_timepoints_humann"
if (!dir.exists(output_dir)) dir.create(output_dir)

# Set up logging
log_file <- file.path(output_dir, "analysis_log.txt")
sink(log_file, split = TRUE)
cat("Analysis started at:", as.character(Sys.time()), "\n\n")

# ============================================================================
# SECTION 2: DATA PREPARATION
# ============================================================================

# Read the HUMAnN phyloseq object (adjust path if needed)
ps_hm <- readRDS('/home/sonec_iv/RSF_res_4_ps/hm3/RSF_ps_humann_joint.rds')
ps_obj <- ps_hm

# Check and prepare the data (as in original script)
otu_matrix <- otu_table(ps_obj)
if (is.matrix(otu_matrix)) {
  otu_matrix[] <- round(otu_matrix)
  otu_table(ps_obj) <- otu_table(otu_matrix, taxa_are_rows = taxa_are_rows(otu_table(ps_obj)))
} else {
  stop("otu_table is not a matrix.")
}

# Apply prevalence filter
prevalence <- 10/100
detection <- 1/100
taxas <- core_members(ps_obj, detection = detection, prevalence = prevalence, include.lowest = FALSE)
ps_obj <- prune_taxa(taxas, ps_obj)

# Prepare metadata
metadf <- data.frame(sample_data(ps_obj))

# Fix numeric columns
numeric_cols <- c("Age", "Weight", "Height", "BMI")
for(col in numeric_cols) {
  if(col %in% names(metadf)) {
    metadf[[col]] <- as.numeric(metadf[[col]])
  }
}

# Ensure Group and Timepoint are factors with correct levels
metadf$Group <- factor(metadf$Group, levels = c("A", "B", "C"))
metadf$Timepoint <- factor(metadf$Timepoint, levels = c("-1", "-2"))

# Define variables to analyze
variables_to_analyze <- c("BMI_group", "Antibiotics_Last_Year", "Proton_Pump_Inhibitors", "Hypoglycemic_Drugs")

# Keep only variables that exist in metadata and have at least 2 levels
valid_vars <- character(0)
for(v in variables_to_analyze) {
  if(v %in% colnames(metadf)) {
    levels <- unique(metadf[[v]][!is.na(metadf[[v]])])
    if(length(levels) >= 2) {
      valid_vars <- c(valid_vars, v)
    } else {
      cat("Variable", v, "has only", length(levels), "levels. Skipping.\n")
    }
  } else {
    cat("Variable", v, "not found in metadata. Skipping.\n")
  }
}
variables_to_analyze <- valid_vars

cat("\nVariables to analyze:", paste(variables_to_analyze, collapse = ", "), "\n")

# ============================================================================
# SECTION 3: HELPER FUNCTIONS
# ============================================================================

# Function to run a single MaAsLin2 analysis for a given subset and model
run_maaslin_model <- function(data_df, metadata_df, output_prefix, model_type,
                              normalization, transformation, debug = TRUE) {
  # data_df: rows = features, columns = samples (will be transposed)
  # metadata_df: samples × metadata
  # output_prefix: used for output directory name
  # model_type: "LM" or "NEGBIN"
  # normalization: e.g., "NONE", "TSS", "CSS", "TMM"
  # transformation: e.g., "NONE", "CLR"
  
  # Create output directory
  out_dir <- file.path(output_dir, paste0("maaslin2_", output_prefix))
  if (!dir.exists(out_dir)) dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE)
  
  # Transpose: MaAsLin2 expects features as columns, samples as rows
  data_t <- as.data.frame(t(data_df))
  
  # Clean feature names (remove special characters)
  original_names <- colnames(data_t)
  clean_names <- make.names(original_names)
  colnames(data_t) <- clean_names
  name_mapping <- data.frame(original = original_names, clean = clean_names, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  
  if (debug) {
    cat("        Running MaAsLin2 with", ncol(data_t), "features and", nrow(data_t), "samples\n")
  }
  
  # Run MaAsLin2
  fit <- tryCatch({
    Maaslin2(
      input_data = data_t,
      input_metadata = metadata_df,
      output = out_dir,
      fixed_effects = "Timepoint",
      reference = "Timepoint,-1",
      normalization = normalization,
      transform = transformation,
      analysis_method = model_type,
      correction = "BH",
      cores = 4,
      max_significance = 0.1,
      plot_heatmap = FALSE,
      plot_scatter = FALSE
    )
  }, error = function(e) {
    cat("        Error in MaAsLin2:", e$message, "\n")
    return(NULL)
  })
  
  if (is.null(fit)) return(NULL)
  
  results <- fit$results
  if (is.null(results) || nrow(results) == 0) {
    if (debug) cat("        No results returned from MaAsLin2\n")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # Restore original feature names
  results$feature <- name_mapping$original[match(results$feature, name_mapping$clean)]
  
  # Debug: show overall significance distribution
  if (debug) {
    n_total <- nrow(results)
    n_q05 <- sum(results$qval <= 0.05, na.rm = TRUE)
    n_q10 <- sum(results$qval <= 0.10, na.rm = TRUE)
    cat("        Total features tested:", n_total, "\n")
    cat("        Features with q ≤ 0.05:", n_q05, "\n")
    cat("        Features with q ≤ 0.10:", n_q10, "\n")
    if (n_q05 > 0) {
      top_results <- results %>%
        filter(qval <= 0.05) %>%
        arrange(qval) %>%
        head(5)
      cat("        Top significant features (by q):\n")
      print(top_results[, c("feature", "coef", "qval")])
    }
  }
  
  # Filter significant (qval <= 0.05) and |coef| >= 1
  filtered <- results %>%
    filter(qval <= 0.05, abs(coef) >= 1)
  
  if (debug) {
    cat("        Features with q ≤ 0.05 and |coef| ≥ 1:", nrow(filtered), "\n")
  }
  
  if (nrow(filtered) == 0) return(NULL)
  
  # Prepare plot data
  plot_data <- data.frame(
    Species = filtered$feature,
    Mean_Coeff = filtered$coef,
    signif = case_when(
      filtered$qval <= 0.001 ~ "***",
      filtered$qval <= 0.01 ~ "**",
      filtered$qval <= 0.05 ~ "*",
      TRUE ~ ""
    ),
    Group = metadata_df$Group[1],  # All rows have same group because we subset by group
    Direction = ifelse(filtered$coef > 0, "Enriched_in_-2", "Decreased_in_-2")
  )
  
  return(plot_data)
}

# Function to create combined barplot (identical to DESeq2 version)
create_combined_plot <- function(combined_data, title, output_dir, filename,
                                 group_colors, group_colors_light) {
  if (nrow(combined_data) == 0) {
    cat("        No data to combine\n")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  cat("        DEBUG - Creating combined plot\n")
  cat("        - Input rows:", nrow(combined_data), "\n")
  cat("        - Unique species:", length(unique(combined_data$Species)), "\n")
  cat("        - Groups present:", paste(unique(combined_data$Group), collapse = ", "), "\n")
  
  # Separate positive and negative coefficients
  pos_data <- combined_data %>% filter(Mean_Coeff > 0)
  neg_data <- combined_data %>% filter(Mean_Coeff < 0)
  
  # Top 20 positive by descending coefficient
  if (nrow(pos_data) > 0) {
    pos_ranking <- pos_data %>%
      group_by(Species) %>%
      summarise(max_coef = max(Mean_Coeff), .groups = "drop") %>%
      arrange(desc(max_coef)) %>%
      dplyr::slice_head(n = 20)
    pos_species <- pos_ranking$Species
  } else {
    pos_species <- character(0)
  }
  
  # Top 20 negative by ascending coefficient (most negative first)
  if (nrow(neg_data) > 0) {
    neg_ranking <- neg_data %>%
      group_by(Species) %>%
      summarise(min_coef = min(Mean_Coeff), .groups = "drop") %>%
      arrange(min_coef) %>%
      dplyr::slice_head(n = 20)
    neg_species <- neg_ranking$Species
  } else {
    neg_species <- character(0)
  }
  
  # Combine selected species (up to 40)
  selected_species <- unique(c(pos_species, neg_species))
  combined_data <- combined_data %>% filter(Species %in% selected_species)
  
  # Ordering: positive descending, then negative ascending (most negative last), then reverse
  if (length(pos_species) > 0) {
    pos_ordered <- pos_data %>%
      filter(Species %in% pos_species) %>%
      group_by(Species) %>%
      summarise(max_fc = max(Mean_Coeff)) %>%
      arrange(desc(max_fc)) %>%
      pull(Species)
  } else {
    pos_ordered <- character(0)
  }
  
  if (length(neg_species) > 0) {
    neg_ordered <- neg_data %>%
      filter(Species %in% neg_species) %>%
      group_by(Species) %>%
      summarise(min_fc = min(Mean_Coeff)) %>%
      arrange(min_fc) %>%
      pull(Species)
  } else {
    neg_ordered <- character(0)
  }
  
  # Ensure uniqueness
  pos_unique <- unique(pos_ordered)
  neg_unique <- setdiff(unique(neg_ordered), pos_unique)
  species_order <- c(pos_unique, neg_unique)
  # Reverse so most positive at top
  species_order <- rev(species_order)
  
  # Apply factor levels
  combined_data <- combined_data %>%
    mutate(Species = factor(Species, levels = species_order))
  
  # Add fill group
  combined_data <- combined_data %>%
    mutate(FillGroup = paste(Group, Direction, sep = "_"))
  
  # Define colors
  color_palette <- c()
  for (g in c("A", "B", "C")) {
    reg <- if(g == "A") "#FF0000" else if(g == "B") "#0000FF" else "#00FF00"
    light <- if(g == "A") "#FF9999" else if(g == "B") "#9999FF" else "#99FF99"
    color_palette[paste(g, "Enriched_in_-2", sep = "_")] <- reg
    color_palette[paste(g, "Decreased_in_-2", sep = "_")] <- light
  }
  
  # Create plot
  p <- ggplot(combined_data, aes(x = Species, y = Mean_Coeff, fill = FillGroup)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), width = 0.8) +
    geom_text(aes(label = signif,
                  y = ifelse(Mean_Coeff >= 0,
                             Mean_Coeff + 0.1 * max(abs(combined_data$Mean_Coeff)),
                             Mean_Coeff - 0.1 * max(abs(combined_data$Mean_Coeff)))),
              position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), size = 3, vjust = 0.5) +
    scale_fill_manual(
      name = "Group & Direction",
      values = color_palette,
      labels = c("A_Enriched_in_-2" = "Group A (enriched in -2)",
                 "A_Decreased_in_-2" = "Group A (decreased in -2)",
                 "B_Enriched_in_-2" = "Group B (enriched in -2)",
                 "B_Decreased_in_-2" = "Group B (decreased in -2)",
                 "C_Enriched_in_-2" = "Group C (enriched in -2)",
                 "C_Decreased_in_-2" = "Group C (decreased in -2)")
    ) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
    coord_flip() +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      axis.text.y = element_text(face = "italic", size = 10, colour = 'black'),
      axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12, colour = 'black'),
      axis.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
      plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
      legend.position = "top",
      legend.text = element_text(size = 12),
      legend.title = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold"),
      plot.margin = margin(1, 1, 1, 1, "cm")
    ) +
    labs(
      title = paste("Timepoint comparisons (-2 vs -1):", title),
      x = "Species",
      y = "Coefficient (Timepoint -2 vs -1)\n(positive = enriched in -2, negative = decreased in -2)"
    )
  
  # Save plot
  n_species <- length(unique(combined_data$Species))
  plot_height <- max(8, n_species * 0.4)
  plot_width <- 16
  ggsave(file.path(output_dir, paste0("plot_", filename, "_combined.pdf")),
         p, width = plot_width, height = plot_height, limitsize = FALSE)
  ggsave(file.path(output_dir, paste0("plot_", filename, "_combined.png")),
         p, width = plot_width, height = plot_height, dpi = 300, limitsize = FALSE)
  
  cat("        ✓ Combined plot saved with", n_species, "species (", nrow(combined_data), "bars)\n")
  return(p)
}

# ============================================================================
# SECTION 4: PREPARE DATA FOR DIFFERENT MODELS
# ============================================================================

# Raw data (for NEGBIN)
data_raw <- as.data.frame(otu_table(ps_obj))

# Compositional data (for LM with CLR)
ps_comp <- microbiome::transform(ps_obj, 'compositional')
data_comp <- as.data.frame(otu_table(ps_comp))

# Metadata is the same for both
metadata_all <- metadf

# ============================================================================
# SECTION 5: MAIN ANALYSIS LOOP
# ============================================================================

# Define groups
groups <- c("A", "B", "C")
min_samples_per_timepoint <- 2  # at least 2 samples per timepoint for a comparison

# Define model configurations
models <- list(
  # LM with CLR transformation
  list(name = "LM_NONE_CLR", model_type = "LM", data = data_comp, metadata = metadata_all,
       normalization = "NONE", transformation = "CLR"),
  list(name = "LM_TSS_CLR", model_type = "LM", data = data_comp, metadata = metadata_all,
       normalization = "TSS", transformation = "CLR"),
  # NEGBIN with raw counts
  list(name = "NEGBIN_CSS", model_type = "NEGBIN", data = data_raw, metadata = metadata_all,
       normalization = "CSS", transformation = "NONE"),
  list(name = "NEGBIN_TMM", model_type = "NEGBIN", data = data_raw, metadata = metadata_all,
       normalization = "TMM", transformation = "NONE")
)

# For each variable in the list
for (variable in variables_to_analyze) {
  cat("\n", strrep("=", 60), "\n")
  cat("Variable:", variable, "\n")
  cat(strrep("=", 60), "\n")
  
  # Get levels of this variable from metadata
  var_levels <- unique(metadf[[variable]][!is.na(metadf[[variable]])])
  cat("Levels:", paste(var_levels, collapse = ", "), "\n")
  
  # For each level of the variable
  for (var_level in var_levels) {
    cat("\n  Level:", var_level, "\n")
    
    # Determine indices for this level
    idx_level <- which(metadf[[variable]] == var_level & !is.na(metadf[[variable]]))
    n_total_samples <- length(idx_level)
    cat("    Total samples in this level:", n_total_samples, "\n")
    
    if (n_total_samples < 3) {
      cat("    Insufficient samples for this level (<3). Skipping.\n")
      next
    }
    
    # For each model, collect results across groups
    for (model in models) {
      model_name <- model$name
      cat("\n    Model:", model_name, "\n")
      
      # Subset data to this level
      data_model <- model$data[, idx_level, drop = FALSE]
      metadata_model <- model$metadata[idx_level, , drop = FALSE]
      
      # Remove features with zero total count in this subset (important for NEGBIN)
      non_zero <- rowSums(data_model) > 0
      data_model <- data_model[non_zero, , drop = FALSE]
      cat("        Features after zero removal:", nrow(data_model), "\n")
      
      if (nrow(data_model) == 0) {
        cat("        No features remain after zero removal. Skipping.\n")
        next
      }
      
      # Collect results from each group
      group_results <- list()
      for (group in groups) {
        cat("      Group:", group, "\n")
        idx_group <- which(metadata_model$Group == group)
        n_group <- length(idx_group)
        cat("        Samples in group:", n_group, "\n")
        if (n_group < min_samples_per_timepoint * 2) {
          cat("        Insufficient samples for group (<", min_samples_per_timepoint*2, "). Skipping.\n")
          next
        }
        
        # Check timepoint distribution
        timepoint_counts <- table(metadata_model$Timepoint[idx_group])
        cat("        Timepoint distribution: -1 =", timepoint_counts["-1"], ", -2 =", timepoint_counts["-2"], "\n")
        if (any(timepoint_counts < min_samples_per_timepoint)) {
          cat("        Insufficient samples in one timepoint. Skipping.\n")
          next
        }
        
        # Subset data and metadata for this group
        data_group <- data_model[, idx_group, drop = FALSE]
        metadata_group <- metadata_model[idx_group, , drop = FALSE]
        
        # Run MaAsLin2
        output_prefix <- paste(variable, var_level, group, model_name, sep = "_")
        res <- run_maaslin_model(data_group, metadata_group, output_prefix,
                                 model_type = model$model_type,
                                 normalization = model$normalization,
                                 transformation = model$transformation,
                                 debug = TRUE)
        if (!is.null(res)) {
          group_results[[group]] <- res
          cat("        Found", nrow(res), "significant species (q ≤ 0.05, |coef| ≥ 1)\n")
        } else {
          cat("        No significant species found\n")
        }
      }
      
      # Combine results from all groups for this model and level
      combined_data <- bind_rows(group_results)
      if (nrow(combined_data) > 0) {
        clean_name <- gsub("[^a-zA-Z0-9]", "_", paste(variable, var_level, model_name, sep = "_"))
        title <- paste(variable, "-", var_level, "(", model_name, ")")
        create_combined_plot(combined_data, title, output_dir, clean_name,
                             group_colors = c("A" = "#FF0000", "B" = "#0000FF", "C" = "#00FF00"),
                             group_colors_light = c("A" = "#FF9999", "B" = "#9999FF", "C" = "#99FF99"))
      } else {
        cat("      No data for model", model_name, "at level", var_level, "\n")
      }
    }
  }
}

cat("\nAnalysis completed at:", as.character(Sys.time()), "\n")
sink()
```
# no grouping by categorical_vars
```{r}
# ============================================================================
# GLOBAL TIMEPOINT COMPARISON PLOTS (MaAsLin2) - All Groups Together
# ============================================================================
# This script analyzes HUMAnN data with MaAsLin2 to compare timepoints (-2 vs -1)
# across all samples, stratified by Group (A, B, C). It creates combined barplots
# showing significant features for all groups in the same plot.
# Features:
# - LM model: NONE/TSS normalization + CLR transformation (compositional data)
# - NEGBIN model: CSS/TMM normalization (raw counts)
# - Top 20 positive and top 20 negative coefficients (up to 40 total)
# - Ordered: most positive at top, most negative at bottom
# - Legend: "Enriched in -2" (positive coefficient, regular colors) and "Decreased in -2" (negative coefficient, light colors)
# ============================================================================

# Load required libraries
library(phyloseq)
library(microbiome)
library(Maaslin2)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)
library(stringr)

# ============================================================================
# SECTION 1: SETUP
# ============================================================================

cat("Starting global timepoint analysis for HUMAnN data...\n")

# Create output directory
output_dir <- "global_timepoints_humann"
if (!dir.exists(output_dir)) dir.create(output_dir)

# Set up logging
log_file <- file.path(output_dir, "analysis_log.txt")
sink(log_file, split = TRUE)
cat("Analysis started at:", as.character(Sys.time()), "\n\n")

# ============================================================================
# SECTION 2: DATA PREPARATION
# ============================================================================

# Read the HUMAnN phyloseq object (adjust path if needed)
ps_hm <- readRDS('/home/sonec_iv/RSF_res_4_ps/hm3/RSF_ps_humann_joint.rds')
ps_obj <- ps_hm

# Check and prepare the data
otu_matrix <- otu_table(ps_obj)
if (is.matrix(otu_matrix)) {
  otu_matrix[] <- round(otu_matrix)
  otu_table(ps_obj) <- otu_table(otu_matrix, taxa_are_rows = taxa_are_rows(otu_table(ps_obj)))
} else {
  stop("otu_table is not a matrix.")
}

# Apply prevalence filter
prevalence <- 10/100
detection <- 1/100
taxas <- core_members(ps_obj, detection = detection, prevalence = prevalence, include.lowest = FALSE)
ps_obj <- prune_taxa(taxas, ps_obj)

# Prepare metadata
metadf <- data.frame(sample_data(ps_obj))

# Fix numeric columns
numeric_cols <- c("Age", "Weight", "Height", "BMI")
for(col in numeric_cols) {
  if(col %in% names(metadf)) {
    metadf[[col]] <- as.numeric(metadf[[col]])
  }
}

# Ensure Group and Timepoint are factors with correct levels
metadf$Group <- factor(metadf$Group, levels = c("A", "B", "C"))
metadf$Timepoint <- factor(metadf$Timepoint, levels = c("-1", "-2"))

# Define groups
groups <- c("A", "B", "C")
min_samples_per_timepoint <- 2  # at least 2 samples per timepoint for a comparison

# ============================================================================
# SECTION 3: PREPARE DATA FOR MODELS
# ============================================================================

# Raw data (for NEGBIN)
data_raw <- as.data.frame(otu_table(ps_obj))

# Compositional data (for LM with CLR)
ps_comp <- microbiome::transform(ps_obj, 'compositional')
data_comp <- as.data.frame(otu_table(ps_comp))

# Metadata is the same for both
metadata_all <- metadf

# ============================================================================
# SECTION 4: HELPER FUNCTIONS
# ============================================================================

# Function to run a single MaAsLin2 analysis for a given subset and model
run_maaslin_model <- function(data_df, metadata_df, output_prefix, model_type,
                              normalization, transformation, debug = TRUE) {
  # Create output directory
  out_dir <- file.path(output_dir, paste0("maaslin2_", output_prefix))
  if (!dir.exists(out_dir)) dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE)
  
  # Transpose: MaAsLin2 expects features as columns, samples as rows
  data_t <- as.data.frame(t(data_df))
  
  # Clean feature names (remove special characters)
  original_names <- colnames(data_t)
  clean_names <- make.names(original_names)
  colnames(data_t) <- clean_names
  name_mapping <- data.frame(original = original_names, clean = clean_names, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  
  if (debug) {
    cat("        Running MaAsLin2 with", ncol(data_t), "features and", nrow(data_t), "samples\n")
  }
  
  # Run MaAsLin2
  fit <- tryCatch({
    Maaslin2(
      input_data = data_t,
      input_metadata = metadata_df,
      output = out_dir,
      fixed_effects = "Timepoint",
      reference = "Timepoint,-1",
      normalization = normalization,
      transform = transformation,
      analysis_method = model_type,
      correction = "BH",
      cores = 4,
      max_significance = 0.1,
      plot_heatmap = FALSE,
      plot_scatter = FALSE
    )
  }, error = function(e) {
    cat("        Error in MaAsLin2:", e$message, "\n")
    return(NULL)
  })
  
  if (is.null(fit)) return(NULL)
  
  results <- fit$results
  if (is.null(results) || nrow(results) == 0) {
    if (debug) cat("        No results returned from MaAsLin2\n")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # Restore original feature names
  results$feature <- name_mapping$original[match(results$feature, name_mapping$clean)]
  
  # Debug: show overall significance distribution
  if (debug) {
    n_total <- nrow(results)
    n_q05 <- sum(results$qval <= 0.05, na.rm = TRUE)
    n_q10 <- sum(results$qval <= 0.10, na.rm = TRUE)
    cat("        Total features tested:", n_total, "\n")
    cat("        Features with q ≤ 0.05:", n_q05, "\n")
    cat("        Features with q ≤ 0.10:", n_q10, "\n")
    if (n_q05 > 0) {
      top_results <- results %>%
        filter(qval <= 0.05) %>%
        arrange(qval) %>%
        head(5)
      cat("        Top significant features (by q):\n")
      print(top_results[, c("feature", "coef", "qval")])
    }
  }
  
  # Filter significant (qval <= 0.05) and |coef| >= 1
  filtered <- results %>%
    filter(qval <= 0.05, abs(coef) >= 1)
  
  if (debug) {
    cat("        Features with q ≤ 0.05 and |coef| ≥ 1:", nrow(filtered), "\n")
  }
  
  if (nrow(filtered) == 0) return(NULL)
  
  # Prepare plot data
  plot_data <- data.frame(
    Species = filtered$feature,
    Mean_Coeff = filtered$coef,
    signif = case_when(
      filtered$qval <= 0.001 ~ "***",
      filtered$qval <= 0.01 ~ "**",
      filtered$qval <= 0.05 ~ "*",
      TRUE ~ ""
    ),
    Group = metadata_df$Group[1],  # All rows have same group because we subset by group
    Direction = ifelse(filtered$coef > 0, "Enriched_in_-2", "Decreased_in_-2")
  )
  
  return(plot_data)
}

# Function to create combined barplot (identical to previous)
create_combined_plot <- function(combined_data, title, output_dir, filename,
                                 group_colors, group_colors_light) {
  if (nrow(combined_data) == 0) {
    cat("        No data to combine\n")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  cat("        DEBUG - Creating combined plot\n")
  cat("        - Input rows:", nrow(combined_data), "\n")
  cat("        - Unique species:", length(unique(combined_data$Species)), "\n")
  cat("        - Groups present:", paste(unique(combined_data$Group), collapse = ", "), "\n")
  
  # Separate positive and negative coefficients
  pos_data <- combined_data %>% filter(Mean_Coeff > 0)
  neg_data <- combined_data %>% filter(Mean_Coeff < 0)
  
  # Top 20 positive by descending coefficient
  if (nrow(pos_data) > 0) {
    pos_ranking <- pos_data %>%
      group_by(Species) %>%
      summarise(max_coef = max(Mean_Coeff), .groups = "drop") %>%
      arrange(desc(max_coef)) %>%
      dplyr::slice_head(n = 20)
    pos_species <- pos_ranking$Species
  } else {
    pos_species <- character(0)
  }
  
  # Top 20 negative by ascending coefficient (most negative first)
  if (nrow(neg_data) > 0) {
    neg_ranking <- neg_data %>%
      group_by(Species) %>%
      summarise(min_coef = min(Mean_Coeff), .groups = "drop") %>%
      arrange(min_coef) %>%
      dplyr::slice_head(n = 20)
    neg_species <- neg_ranking$Species
  } else {
    neg_species <- character(0)
  }
  
  # Combine selected species (up to 40)
  selected_species <- unique(c(pos_species, neg_species))
  combined_data <- combined_data %>% filter(Species %in% selected_species)
  
  # Ordering: positive descending, then negative ascending (most negative last), then reverse
  if (length(pos_species) > 0) {
    pos_ordered <- pos_data %>%
      filter(Species %in% pos_species) %>%
      group_by(Species) %>%
      summarise(max_fc = max(Mean_Coeff)) %>%
      arrange(desc(max_fc)) %>%
      pull(Species)
  } else {
    pos_ordered <- character(0)
  }
  
  if (length(neg_species) > 0) {
    neg_ordered <- neg_data %>%
      filter(Species %in% neg_species) %>%
      group_by(Species) %>%
      summarise(min_fc = min(Mean_Coeff)) %>%
      arrange(min_fc) %>%
      pull(Species)
  } else {
    neg_ordered <- character(0)
  }
  
  # Ensure uniqueness
  pos_unique <- unique(pos_ordered)
  neg_unique <- setdiff(unique(neg_ordered), pos_unique)
  species_order <- c(pos_unique, neg_unique)
  # Reverse so most positive at top
  species_order <- rev(species_order)
  
  # Apply factor levels
  combined_data <- combined_data %>%
    mutate(Species = factor(Species, levels = species_order))
  
  # Add fill group
  combined_data <- combined_data %>%
    mutate(FillGroup = paste(Group, Direction, sep = "_"))
  
  # Define colors
  color_palette <- c()
  for (g in c("A", "B", "C")) {
    reg <- if(g == "A") "#FF0000" else if(g == "B") "#0000FF" else "#00FF00"
    light <- if(g == "A") "#FF9999" else if(g == "B") "#9999FF" else "#99FF99"
    color_palette[paste(g, "Enriched_in_-2", sep = "_")] <- reg
    color_palette[paste(g, "Decreased_in_-2", sep = "_")] <- light
  }
  
  # Create plot
  p <- ggplot(combined_data, aes(x = Species, y = Mean_Coeff, fill = FillGroup)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), width = 0.8) +
    geom_text(aes(label = signif,
                  y = ifelse(Mean_Coeff >= 0,
                             Mean_Coeff + 0.1 * max(abs(combined_data$Mean_Coeff)),
                             Mean_Coeff - 0.1 * max(abs(combined_data$Mean_Coeff)))),
              position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), size = 3, vjust = 0.5) +
    scale_fill_manual(
      name = "Group & Direction",
      values = color_palette,
      labels = c("A_Enriched_in_-2" = "Group A (enriched in -2)",
                 "A_Decreased_in_-2" = "Group A (decreased in -2)",
                 "B_Enriched_in_-2" = "Group B (enriched in -2)",
                 "B_Decreased_in_-2" = "Group B (decreased in -2)",
                 "C_Enriched_in_-2" = "Group C (enriched in -2)",
                 "C_Decreased_in_-2" = "Group C (decreased in -2)")
    ) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
    coord_flip() +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      axis.text.y = element_text(face = "italic", size = 10, colour = 'black'),
      axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12, colour = 'black'),
      axis.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
      plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
      legend.position = "top",
      legend.text = element_text(size = 12),
      legend.title = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold"),
      plot.margin = margin(1, 1, 1, 1, "cm")
    ) +
    labs(
      title = paste("Timepoint comparisons (-2 vs -1) – Global:", title),
      x = "Species",
      y = "Coefficient (Timepoint -2 vs -1)\n(positive = enriched in -2, negative = decreased in -2)"
    )
  
  # Save plot
  n_species <- length(unique(combined_data$Species))
  plot_height <- max(8, n_species * 0.4)
  plot_width <- 16
  ggsave(file.path(output_dir, paste0("plot_", filename, "_combined.pdf")),
         p, width = plot_width, height = plot_height, limitsize = FALSE)
  ggsave(file.path(output_dir, paste0("plot_", filename, "_combined.png")),
         p, width = plot_width, height = plot_height, dpi = 300, limitsize = FALSE)
  
  cat("        ✓ Combined plot saved with", n_species, "species (", nrow(combined_data), "bars)\n")
  return(p)
}

# ============================================================================
# SECTION 5: MAIN ANALYSIS – FOR EACH GROUP, RUN ALL MODELS
# ============================================================================

# Define model configurations
models <- list(
  # LM with CLR transformation
  list(name = "LM_NONE_CLR", model_type = "LM", data = data_comp, 
       normalization = "NONE", transformation = "CLR"),
  list(name = "LM_TSS_CLR", model_type = "LM", data = data_comp,
       normalization = "TSS", transformation = "CLR"),
  # NEGBIN with raw counts
  list(name = "NEGBIN_CSS", model_type = "NEGBIN", data = data_raw,
       normalization = "CSS", transformation = "NONE"),
  list(name = "NEGBIN_TMM", model_type = "NEGBIN", data = data_raw,
       normalization = "TMM", transformation = "NONE")
)

# For each model, collect results across groups
for (model in models) {
  model_name <- model$name
  cat("\n", strrep("=", 60), "\n")
  cat("Model:", model_name, "\n")
  cat(strrep("=", 60), "\n")
  
  # Use the appropriate data for this model (already set in model)
  data_all <- model$data
  metadata_all <- metadf
  
  # Check that we have enough samples overall
  cat("  Total samples:", ncol(data_all), "\n")
  
  # Collect results per group
  group_results <- list()
  
  for (group in groups) {
    cat("\n  Group:", group, "\n")
    idx_group <- which(metadata_all$Group == group)
    n_group <- length(idx_group)
    cat("    Samples in group:", n_group, "\n")
    if (n_group < min_samples_per_timepoint * 2) {
      cat("    Insufficient samples for group (<", min_samples_per_timepoint*2, "). Skipping.\n")
      next
    }
    
    # Check timepoint distribution
    timepoint_counts <- table(metadata_all$Timepoint[idx_group])
    cat("    Timepoint distribution: -1 =", timepoint_counts["-1"], ", -2 =", timepoint_counts["-2"], "\n")
    if (any(timepoint_counts < min_samples_per_timepoint)) {
      cat("    Insufficient samples in one timepoint. Skipping.\n")
      next
    }
    
    # Subset data and metadata for this group
    data_group <- data_all[, idx_group, drop = FALSE]
    metadata_group <- metadata_all[idx_group, , drop = FALSE]
    
    # Remove features with zero total count in this subset (important for NEGBIN)
    non_zero <- rowSums(data_group) > 0
    data_group <- data_group[non_zero, , drop = FALSE]
    cat("    Features after zero removal:", nrow(data_group), "\n")
    
    if (nrow(data_group) == 0) {
      cat("    No features remain after zero removal. Skipping.\n")
      next
    }
    
    # Run MaAsLin2
    output_prefix <- paste("group", group, model_name, sep = "_")
    res <- run_maaslin_model(data_group, metadata_group, output_prefix,
                             model_type = model$model_type,
                             normalization = model$normalization,
                             transformation = model$transformation,
                             debug = TRUE)
    if (!is.null(res)) {
      group_results[[group]] <- res
      cat("    Found", nrow(res), "significant species (q ≤ 0.05, |coef| ≥ 1)\n")
    } else {
      cat("    No significant species found\n")
    }
  }
  
  # Combine results from all groups for this model
  combined_data <- bind_rows(group_results)
  if (nrow(combined_data) > 0) {
    clean_name <- gsub("[^a-zA-Z0-9]", "_", model_name)
    title <- paste("Global Analysis -", model_name)
    create_combined_plot(combined_data, title, output_dir, clean_name,
                         group_colors = c("A" = "#FF0000", "B" = "#0000FF", "C" = "#00FF00"),
                         group_colors_light = c("A" = "#FF9999", "B" = "#9999FF", "C" = "#99FF99"))
  } else {
    cat("\n  No data for model", model_name, "\n")
  }
}

cat("\nAnalysis completed at:", as.character(Sys.time()), "\n")
sink()
```

# global negbin tmm only
```{r}
# ============================================================================
# GLOBAL TIMEPOINT COMPARISON PLOT (MaAsLin2 - NEGBIN TMM) with Debug
# ============================================================================
# This script analyzes HUMAnN data with MaAsLin2 (NEGBIN + TMM normalization)
# to compare timepoints (-2 vs -1) across all samples, stratified by Group (A, B, C).
# It creates a combined barplot showing significant features for all groups.
# Debugging: detailed output of significant features per group.
# ============================================================================

# Load required libraries
library(phyloseq)
library(microbiome)
library(Maaslin2)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)
library(stringr)

# ============================================================================
# SECTION 1: SETUP
# ============================================================================

cat("Starting NEGBIN TMM global timepoint analysis with debug...\n")

# Create output directory
output_dir <- "global_timepoints_negbin_tmm"
if (!dir.exists(output_dir)) dir.create(output_dir)

# Set up logging (console + file)
log_file <- file.path(output_dir, "analysis_log.txt")
sink(log_file, split = TRUE)
cat("Analysis started at:", as.character(Sys.time()), "\n\n")

# Separate debug file for detailed info
debug_file <- file.path(output_dir, "analysis_debug.txt")
cat("Detailed debug output will be written to:", debug_file, "\n")

# ============================================================================
# SECTION 2: DATA PREPARATION
# ============================================================================

# Read the HUMAnN phyloseq object (adjust path if needed)
ps_hm <- readRDS('/home/sonec_iv/RSF_res_4_ps/hm3/RSF_ps_humann_joint.rds')
ps_obj <- ps_hm

# Check and prepare the data
otu_matrix <- otu_table(ps_obj)
if (is.matrix(otu_matrix)) {
  otu_matrix[] <- round(otu_matrix)
  otu_table(ps_obj) <- otu_table(otu_matrix, taxa_are_rows = taxa_are_rows(otu_table(ps_obj)))
} else {
  stop("otu_table is not a matrix.")
}

# Apply prevalence filter
prevalence <- 10/100
detection <- 1/100
taxas <- core_members(ps_obj, detection = detection, prevalence = prevalence, include.lowest = FALSE)
ps_obj <- prune_taxa(taxas, ps_obj)

# Prepare metadata
metadf <- data.frame(sample_data(ps_obj))

# Fix numeric columns
numeric_cols <- c("Age", "Weight", "Height", "BMI")
for(col in numeric_cols) {
  if(col %in% names(metadf)) {
    metadf[[col]] <- as.numeric(metadf[[col]])
  }
}

# Ensure Group and Timepoint are factors with correct levels
metadf$Group <- factor(metadf$Group, levels = c("A", "B", "C"))
metadf$Timepoint <- factor(metadf$Timepoint, levels = c("-1", "-2"))

# Define groups
groups <- c("A", "B", "C")
min_samples_per_timepoint <- 2  # at least 2 samples per timepoint for a comparison

# ============================================================================
# SECTION 3: PREPARE RAW DATA (for NEGBIN)
# ============================================================================

data_raw <- as.data.frame(otu_table(ps_obj))
metadata_all <- metadf

# ============================================================================
# SECTION 4: HELPER FUNCTIONS
# ============================================================================

# Function to run a single MaAsLin2 analysis for a given subset and model
run_maaslin_model <- function(data_df, metadata_df, output_prefix, debug = TRUE) {
  # Create output directory
  out_dir <- file.path(output_dir, paste0("maaslin2_", output_prefix))
  if (!dir.exists(out_dir)) dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE)
  
  # Transpose: MaAsLin2 expects features as columns, samples as rows
  data_t <- as.data.frame(t(data_df))
  
  # Clean feature names (remove special characters)
  original_names <- colnames(data_t)
  clean_names <- make.names(original_names)
  colnames(data_t) <- clean_names
  name_mapping <- data.frame(original = original_names, clean = clean_names, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  
  if (debug) {
    cat("        Running MaAsLin2 with", ncol(data_t), "features and", nrow(data_t), "samples\n")
  }
  
  # Run MaAsLin2
  fit <- tryCatch({
    Maaslin2(
      input_data = data_t,
      input_metadata = metadata_df,
      output = out_dir,
      fixed_effects = "Timepoint",
      reference = "Timepoint,-1",
      normalization = "TMM",
      transform = "NONE",
      analysis_method = "NEGBIN",
      correction = "BH",
      cores = 4,
      max_significance = 0.1,
      plot_heatmap = FALSE,
      plot_scatter = FALSE
    )
  }, error = function(e) {
    cat("        Error in MaAsLin2:", e$message, "\n")
    return(NULL)
  })
  
  if (is.null(fit)) return(NULL)
  
  results <- fit$results
  if (is.null(results) || nrow(results) == 0) {
    if (debug) cat("        No results returned from MaAsLin2\n")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # Restore original feature names
  results$feature <- name_mapping$original[match(results$feature, name_mapping$clean)]
  
  # Debug: show overall significance distribution
  if (debug) {
    n_total <- nrow(results)
    n_q05 <- sum(results$qval <= 0.05, na.rm = TRUE)
    n_q10 <- sum(results$qval <= 0.10, na.rm = TRUE)
    cat("        Total features tested:", n_total, "\n")
    cat("        Features with q ≤ 0.05:", n_q05, "\n")
    cat("        Features with q ≤ 0.10:", n_q10, "\n")
    if (n_q05 > 0) {
      top_results <- results %>%
        filter(qval <= 0.05) %>%
        arrange(qval) %>%
        head(5)
      cat("        Top significant features (by q):\n")
      print(top_results[, c("feature", "coef", "qval")])
    }
  }
  
  # Filter significant (qval <= 0.05) and |coef| >= 1
  filtered <- results %>%
    filter(qval <= 0.05, abs(coef) >= 1)
  
  if (debug) {
    cat("        Features with q ≤ 0.05 and |coef| ≥ 1:", nrow(filtered), "\n")
    if (nrow(filtered) > 0) {
      cat("        Distribution of coefficients among these:\n")
      print(summary(filtered$coef))
    }
  }
  
  # Also save the full significant results (with relaxed filter) for debugging
  if (nrow(results) > 0) {
    full_sig <- results %>% filter(qval <= 0.05)
    if (nrow(full_sig) > 0) {
      debug_csv <- file.path(out_dir, "all_significant_q05.csv")
      write.csv(full_sig, debug_csv, row.names = FALSE)
      cat("        Saved all q ≤ 0.05 results to:", debug_csv, "\n")
    }
  }
  
  if (nrow(filtered) == 0) return(NULL)
  
  # Prepare plot data
  plot_data <- data.frame(
    Species = filtered$feature,
    Mean_Coeff = filtered$coef,
    signif = case_when(
      filtered$qval <= 0.001 ~ "***",
      filtered$qval <= 0.01 ~ "**",
      filtered$qval <= 0.05 ~ "*",
      TRUE ~ ""
    ),
    Group = metadata_df$Group[1],
    Direction = ifelse(filtered$coef > 0, "Enriched_in_-2", "Decreased_in_-2")
  )
  
  return(plot_data)
}

# Function to create combined barplot with 2‑column legend
create_combined_plot <- function(combined_data, title, output_dir, filename,
                                 group_colors, group_colors_light) {
  if (nrow(combined_data) == 0) {
    cat("        No data to combine\n")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  cat("        DEBUG - Creating combined plot\n")
  cat("        - Input rows:", nrow(combined_data), "\n")
  cat("        - Unique species:", length(unique(combined_data$Species)), "\n")
  cat("        - Groups present:", paste(unique(combined_data$Group), collapse = ", "), "\n")
  
  # Separate positive and negative coefficients
  pos_data <- combined_data %>% filter(Mean_Coeff > 0)
  neg_data <- combined_data %>% filter(Mean_Coeff < 0)
  
  cat("        - Positive features (total):", nrow(pos_data), "\n")
  cat("        - Negative features (total):", nrow(neg_data), "\n")
  
  # Top 20 positive by descending coefficient
  if (nrow(pos_data) > 0) {
    pos_ranking <- pos_data %>%
      group_by(Species) %>%
      summarise(max_coef = max(Mean_Coeff), .groups = "drop") %>%
      arrange(desc(max_coef)) %>%
      dplyr::slice_head(n = 20)
    pos_species <- pos_ranking$Species
  } else {
    pos_species <- character(0)
  }
  
  # Top 20 negative by ascending coefficient (most negative first)
  if (nrow(neg_data) > 0) {
    neg_ranking <- neg_data %>%
      group_by(Species) %>%
      summarise(min_coef = min(Mean_Coeff), .groups = "drop") %>%
      arrange(min_coef) %>%
      dplyr::slice_head(n = 20)
    neg_species <- neg_ranking$Species
  } else {
    neg_species <- character(0)
  }
  
  cat("        - Positive species selected:", length(pos_species), "\n")
  cat("        - Negative species selected:", length(neg_species), "\n")
  
  # Combine selected species (up to 40)
  selected_species <- unique(c(pos_species, neg_species))
  combined_data <- combined_data %>% filter(Species %in% selected_species)
  
  # Ordering: positive descending, then negative ascending (most negative last), then reverse
  if (length(pos_species) > 0) {
    pos_ordered <- pos_data %>%
      filter(Species %in% pos_species) %>%
      group_by(Species) %>%
      summarise(max_fc = max(Mean_Coeff)) %>%
      arrange(desc(max_fc)) %>%
      pull(Species)
  } else {
    pos_ordered <- character(0)
  }
  
  if (length(neg_species) > 0) {
    neg_ordered <- neg_data %>%
      filter(Species %in% neg_species) %>%
      group_by(Species) %>%
      summarise(min_fc = min(Mean_Coeff)) %>%
      arrange(min_fc) %>%
      pull(Species)
  } else {
    neg_ordered <- character(0)
  }
  
  # Ensure uniqueness
  pos_unique <- unique(pos_ordered)
  neg_unique <- setdiff(unique(neg_ordered), pos_unique)
  species_order <- c(pos_unique, neg_unique)
  # Reverse so most positive at top
  species_order <- rev(species_order)
  
  # Apply factor levels
  combined_data <- combined_data %>%
    mutate(Species = factor(Species, levels = species_order))
  
  # Add fill group
  combined_data <- combined_data %>%
    mutate(FillGroup = paste(Group, Direction, sep = "_"))
  
  # Define colors
  color_palette <- c()
  for (g in c("A", "B", "C")) {
    reg <- if(g == "A") "#FF0000" else if(g == "B") "#0000FF" else "#00FF00"
    light <- if(g == "A") "#FF9999" else if(g == "B") "#9999FF" else "#99FF99"
    color_palette[paste(g, "Enriched_in_-2", sep = "_")] <- reg
    color_palette[paste(g, "Decreased_in_-2", sep = "_")] <- light
  }
  
  # Create plot with legend in 2 columns
  p <- ggplot(combined_data, aes(x = Species, y = Mean_Coeff, fill = FillGroup)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), width = 0.8) +
    geom_text(aes(label = signif,
                  y = ifelse(Mean_Coeff >= 0,
                             Mean_Coeff + 0.1 * max(abs(combined_data$Mean_Coeff)),
                             Mean_Coeff - 0.1 * max(abs(combined_data$Mean_Coeff)))),
              position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), size = 3, vjust = 0.5) +
    scale_fill_manual(
      name = "Group & Direction",
      values = color_palette,
      labels = c("A_Enriched_in_-2" = "Group A (enriched in -2)",
                 "A_Decreased_in_-2" = "Group A (decreased in -2)",
                 "B_Enriched_in_-2" = "Group B (enriched in -2)",
                 "B_Decreased_in_-2" = "Group B (decreased in -2)",
                 "C_Enriched_in_-2" = "Group C (enriched in -2)",
                 "C_Decreased_in_-2" = "Group C (decreased in -2)")
    ) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
    coord_flip() +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      axis.text.y = element_text(face = "italic", size = 10, colour = 'black'),
      axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12, colour = 'black'),
      axis.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
      plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
      legend.position = "top",
      legend.text = element_text(size = 12),
      legend.title = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold"),
      legend.box = "horizontal",
      plot.margin = margin(1, 1, 1, 1, "cm")
    ) +
    guides(fill = guide_legend(ncol = 2, byrow = TRUE)) +
    labs(
      title = paste("Timepoint comparisons (-2 vs -1) – Global (NEGBIN TMM)"),
      x = "Species",
      y = "Coefficient (Timepoint -2 vs -1)\n(positive = enriched in -2, negative = decreased in -2)"
    )
  
  # Save plot with increased width
  n_species <- length(unique(combined_data$Species))
  plot_height <- max(8, n_species * 0.4)
  plot_width <- 18
  
  ggsave(file.path(output_dir, paste0("plot_", filename, "_combined.pdf")),
         p, width = plot_width, height = plot_height, limitsize = FALSE)
  ggsave(file.path(output_dir, paste0("plot_", filename, "_combined.png")),
         p, width = plot_width, height = plot_height, dpi = 300, limitsize = FALSE)
  
  cat("        ✓ Combined plot saved with", n_species, "species (", nrow(combined_data), "bars)\n")
  return(p)
}

# ============================================================================
# SECTION 5: MAIN ANALYSIS – NEGBIN TMM FOR ALL GROUPS
# ============================================================================

cat("\n", strrep("=", 60), "\n")
cat("Model: NEGBIN + TMM\n")
cat(strrep("=", 60), "\n")

# Use the raw data for NEGBIN
data_all <- data_raw
metadata_all <- metadf

cat("  Total samples:", ncol(data_all), "\n")

# Collect results per group
group_results <- list()

# Open debug file for detailed output
sink(debug_file, append = FALSE)  # Overwrite previous debug file
cat("=== DETAILED DEBUG OUTPUT FOR EACH GROUP ===\n\n")
sink()  # Back to console and main log

for (group in groups) {
  cat("\n  Group:", group, "\n")
  idx_group <- which(metadata_all$Group == group)
  n_group <- length(idx_group)
  cat("    Samples in group:", n_group, "\n")
  if (n_group < min_samples_per_timepoint * 2) {
    cat("    Insufficient samples for group (<", min_samples_per_timepoint*2, "). Skipping.\n")
    next
  }
  
  # Check timepoint distribution
  timepoint_counts <- table(metadata_all$Timepoint[idx_group])
  cat("    Timepoint distribution: -1 =", timepoint_counts["-1"], ", -2 =", timepoint_counts["-2"], "\n")
  if (any(timepoint_counts < min_samples_per_timepoint)) {
    cat("    Insufficient samples in one timepoint. Skipping.\n")
    next
  }
  
  # Subset data and metadata for this group
  data_group <- data_all[, idx_group, drop = FALSE]
  metadata_group <- metadata_all[idx_group, , drop = FALSE]
  
  # Remove features with zero total count in this subset
  non_zero <- rowSums(data_group) > 0
  data_group <- data_group[non_zero, , drop = FALSE]
  cat("    Features after zero removal:", nrow(data_group), "\n")
  
  if (nrow(data_group) == 0) {
    cat("    No features remain after zero removal. Skipping.\n")
    next
  }
  
  # Run MaAsLin2
  output_prefix <- paste("group", group, "NEGBIN_TMM", sep = "_")
  res <- run_maaslin_model(data_group, metadata_group, output_prefix, debug = TRUE)
  if (!is.null(res)) {
    group_results[[group]] <- res
    cat("    Found", nrow(res), "significant species (q ≤ 0.05, |coef| ≥ 1)\n")
    # Also write to debug file
    sink(debug_file, append = TRUE)
    cat("\n=== Group", group, " ===\n")
    cat("Number of significant (q ≤ 0.05, |coef| ≥ 1):", nrow(res), "\n")
    if (nrow(res) > 0) {
      cat("Positive count:", sum(res$Mean_Coeff > 0), "\n")
      cat("Negative count:", sum(res$Mean_Coeff < 0), "\n")
      cat("Coefficient range:\n")
      print(summary(res$Mean_Coeff))
    }
    sink()
  } else {
    cat("    No significant species found\n")
    sink(debug_file, append = TRUE)
    cat("\n=== Group", group, " ===\n")
    cat("No significant species (q ≤ 0.05, |coef| ≥ 1) found.\n")
    sink()
  }
}

# Combine results from all groups
combined_data <- bind_rows(group_results)
if (nrow(combined_data) > 0) {
  # Write combined summary to debug file
  sink(debug_file, append = TRUE)
  cat("\n=== COMBINED DATA BEFORE PLOTTING ===\n")
  cat("Total rows:", nrow(combined_data), "\n")
  cat("Per group:\n")
  print(table(combined_data$Group))
  cat("Per direction:\n")
  print(table(combined_data$Direction))
  cat("Per group & direction:\n")
  print(table(combined_data$Group, combined_data$Direction))
  sink()
  
  clean_name <- "global_NEGBIN_TMM"
  title <- "Global Analysis - NEGBIN TMM"
  create_combined_plot(combined_data, title, output_dir, clean_name,
                       group_colors = c("A" = "#FF0000", "B" = "#0000FF", "C" = "#00FF00"),
                       group_colors_light = c("A" = "#FF9999", "B" = "#9999FF", "C" = "#99FF99"))
} else {
  cat("\n  No data for NEGBIN TMM model\n")
  sink(debug_file, append = TRUE)
  cat("\n=== NO SIGNIFICANT DATA COMBINED ===\n")
  sink()
}

cat("\nAnalysis completed at:", as.character(Sys.time()), "\n")
sink()
```

# negbin tmm top20 for each group
```{r}
# ============================================================================
# GLOBAL TIMEPOINT COMPARISON PLOT (MaAsLin2 - NEGBIN TMM) – Per‑Group Top 20
# ============================================================================
# This script analyzes HUMAnN data with MaAsLin2 (NEGBIN + TMM normalization)
# to compare timepoints (-2 vs -1) across all samples, stratified by Group (A, B, C).
# It creates a combined barplot showing significant features for all groups.
# For each group, the top 20 positive and top 20 negative coefficients are selected
# (based on coefficient magnitude) and combined.
# ============================================================================

# Load required libraries
library(phyloseq)
library(microbiome)
library(Maaslin2)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)
library(stringr)

# ============================================================================
# SECTION 1: SETUP
# ============================================================================

cat("Starting NEGBIN TMM global timepoint analysis with per‑group top‑20...\n")

# Create output directory
output_dir <- "global_timepoints_negbin_tmm"
if (!dir.exists(output_dir)) dir.create(output_dir)

# Set up logging (console + file)
log_file <- file.path(output_dir, "analysis_log.txt")
sink(log_file, split = TRUE)
cat("Analysis started at:", as.character(Sys.time()), "\n\n")

# Separate debug file for detailed info
debug_file <- file.path(output_dir, "analysis_debug.txt")
cat("Detailed debug output will be written to:", debug_file, "\n")

# ============================================================================
# SECTION 2: DATA PREPARATION
# ============================================================================

# Read the HUMAnN phyloseq object (adjust path if needed)
ps_hm <- readRDS('/home/sonec_iv/RSF_res_4_ps/hm3/RSF_ps_humann_joint.rds')
ps_obj <- ps_hm

# Check and prepare the data
otu_matrix <- otu_table(ps_obj)
if (is.matrix(otu_matrix)) {
  otu_matrix[] <- round(otu_matrix)
  otu_table(ps_obj) <- otu_table(otu_matrix, taxa_are_rows = taxa_are_rows(otu_table(ps_obj)))
} else {
  stop("otu_table is not a matrix.")
}

# Apply prevalence filter
prevalence <- 10/100
detection <- 1/100
taxas <- core_members(ps_obj, detection = detection, prevalence = prevalence, include.lowest = FALSE)
ps_obj <- prune_taxa(taxas, ps_obj)

# Prepare metadata
metadf <- data.frame(sample_data(ps_obj))

# Fix numeric columns
numeric_cols <- c("Age", "Weight", "Height", "BMI")
for(col in numeric_cols) {
  if(col %in% names(metadf)) {
    metadf[[col]] <- as.numeric(metadf[[col]])
  }
}

# Ensure Group and Timepoint are factors with correct levels
metadf$Group <- factor(metadf$Group, levels = c("A", "B", "C"))
metadf$Timepoint <- factor(metadf$Timepoint, levels = c("-1", "-2"))

# Define groups
groups <- c("A", "B", "C")
min_samples_per_timepoint <- 2  # at least 2 samples per timepoint for a comparison

# ============================================================================
# SECTION 3: PREPARE RAW DATA (for NEGBIN)
# ============================================================================

data_raw <- as.data.frame(otu_table(ps_obj))
metadata_all <- metadf

# ============================================================================
# SECTION 4: HELPER FUNCTIONS
# ============================================================================

# Function to run a single MaAsLin2 analysis for a given subset and model
run_maaslin_model <- function(data_df, metadata_df, output_prefix, debug = TRUE) {
  # Create output directory
  out_dir <- file.path(output_dir, paste0("maaslin2_", output_prefix))
  if (!dir.exists(out_dir)) dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE)
  
  # Transpose: MaAsLin2 expects features as columns, samples as rows
  data_t <- as.data.frame(t(data_df))
  
  # Clean feature names (remove special characters)
  original_names <- colnames(data_t)
  clean_names <- make.names(original_names)
  colnames(data_t) <- clean_names
  name_mapping <- data.frame(original = original_names, clean = clean_names, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  
  if (debug) {
    cat("        Running MaAsLin2 with", ncol(data_t), "features and", nrow(data_t), "samples\n")
  }
  
  # Run MaAsLin2
  fit <- tryCatch({
    Maaslin2(
      input_data = data_t,
      input_metadata = metadata_df,
      output = out_dir,
      fixed_effects = "Timepoint",
      reference = "Timepoint,-1",
      normalization = "TMM",
      transform = "NONE",
      analysis_method = "NEGBIN",
      correction = "BH",
      cores = 4,
      max_significance = 0.1,
      plot_heatmap = FALSE,
      plot_scatter = FALSE
    )
  }, error = function(e) {
    cat("        Error in MaAsLin2:", e$message, "\n")
    return(NULL)
  })
  
  if (is.null(fit)) return(NULL)
  
  results <- fit$results
  if (is.null(results) || nrow(results) == 0) {
    if (debug) cat("        No results returned from MaAsLin2\n")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # Restore original feature names
  results$feature <- name_mapping$original[match(results$feature, name_mapping$clean)]
  
  # Debug: show overall significance distribution
  if (debug) {
    n_total <- nrow(results)
    n_q05 <- sum(results$qval <= 0.05, na.rm = TRUE)
    n_q10 <- sum(results$qval <= 0.10, na.rm = TRUE)
    cat("        Total features tested:", n_total, "\n")
    cat("        Features with q ≤ 0.05:", n_q05, "\n")
    cat("        Features with q ≤ 0.10:", n_q10, "\n")
    if (n_q05 > 0) {
      top_results <- results %>%
        filter(qval <= 0.05) %>%
        arrange(qval) %>%
        head(5)
      cat("        Top significant features (by q):\n")
      print(top_results[, c("feature", "coef", "qval")])
    }
  }
  
  # Filter significant (qval <= 0.05) and |coef| >= 1
  filtered <- results %>%
    filter(qval <= 0.05, abs(coef) >= 1)
  
  if (debug) {
    cat("        Features with q ≤ 0.05 and |coef| ≥ 1:", nrow(filtered), "\n")
    if (nrow(filtered) > 0) {
      cat("        Distribution of coefficients among these:\n")
      print(summary(filtered$coef))
    }
  }
  
  # Also save the full significant results (with relaxed filter) for debugging
  if (nrow(results) > 0) {
    full_sig <- results %>% filter(qval <= 0.05)
    if (nrow(full_sig) > 0) {
      debug_csv <- file.path(out_dir, "all_significant_q05.csv")
      write.csv(full_sig, debug_csv, row.names = FALSE)
      cat("        Saved all q ≤ 0.05 results to:", debug_csv, "\n")
    }
  }
  
  if (nrow(filtered) == 0) return(NULL)
  
  # Prepare plot data
  plot_data <- data.frame(
    Species = filtered$feature,
    Mean_Coeff = filtered$coef,
    signif = case_when(
      filtered$qval <= 0.001 ~ "***",
      filtered$qval <= 0.01 ~ "**",
      filtered$qval <= 0.05 ~ "*",
      TRUE ~ ""
    ),
    Group = metadata_df$Group[1],
    Direction = ifelse(filtered$coef > 0, "Enriched_in_-2", "Decreased_in_-2")
  )
  
  return(plot_data)
}

# Function to create combined barplot with 2‑column legend (unchanged)
create_combined_plot <- function(combined_data, title, output_dir, filename,
                                 group_colors, group_colors_light) {
  if (nrow(combined_data) == 0) {
    cat("        No data to combine\n")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  cat("        DEBUG - Creating combined plot\n")
  cat("        - Input rows:", nrow(combined_data), "\n")
  cat("        - Unique species:", length(unique(combined_data$Species)), "\n")
  cat("        - Groups present:", paste(unique(combined_data$Group), collapse = ", "), "\n")
  
  # Separate positive and negative coefficients
  pos_data <- combined_data %>% filter(Mean_Coeff > 0)
  neg_data <- combined_data %>% filter(Mean_Coeff < 0)
  
  cat("        - Positive features (total):", nrow(pos_data), "\n")
  cat("        - Negative features (total):", nrow(neg_data), "\n")
  
  # Top 20 positive by descending coefficient
  if (nrow(pos_data) > 0) {
    pos_ranking <- pos_data %>%
      group_by(Species) %>%
      summarise(max_coef = max(Mean_Coeff), .groups = "drop") %>%
      arrange(desc(max_coef)) %>%
      dplyr::slice_head(n = 20)
    pos_species <- pos_ranking$Species
  } else {
    pos_species <- character(0)
  }
  
  # Top 20 negative by ascending coefficient (most negative first)
  if (nrow(neg_data) > 0) {
    neg_ranking <- neg_data %>%
      group_by(Species) %>%
      summarise(min_coef = min(Mean_Coeff), .groups = "drop") %>%
      arrange(min_coef) %>%
      dplyr::slice_head(n = 20)
    neg_species <- neg_ranking$Species
  } else {
    neg_species <- character(0)
  }
  
  cat("        - Positive species selected:", length(pos_species), "\n")
  cat("        - Negative species selected:", length(neg_species), "\n")
  
  # Combine selected species (up to 40)
  selected_species <- unique(c(pos_species, neg_species))
  combined_data <- combined_data %>% filter(Species %in% selected_species)
  
  # Ordering: positive descending, then negative ascending (most negative last), then reverse
  if (length(pos_species) > 0) {
    pos_ordered <- pos_data %>%
      filter(Species %in% pos_species) %>%
      group_by(Species) %>%
      summarise(max_fc = max(Mean_Coeff)) %>%
      arrange(desc(max_fc)) %>%
      pull(Species)
  } else {
    pos_ordered <- character(0)
  }
  
  if (length(neg_species) > 0) {
    neg_ordered <- neg_data %>%
      filter(Species %in% neg_species) %>%
      group_by(Species) %>%
      summarise(min_fc = min(Mean_Coeff)) %>%
      arrange(min_fc) %>%
      pull(Species)
  } else {
    neg_ordered <- character(0)
  }
  
  # Ensure uniqueness
  pos_unique <- unique(pos_ordered)
  neg_unique <- setdiff(unique(neg_ordered), pos_unique)
  species_order <- c(pos_unique, neg_unique)
  # Reverse so most positive at top
  species_order <- rev(species_order)
  
  # Apply factor levels
  combined_data <- combined_data %>%
    mutate(Species = factor(Species, levels = species_order))
  
  # Add fill group
  combined_data <- combined_data %>%
    mutate(FillGroup = paste(Group, Direction, sep = "_"))
  
  # Define colors
  color_palette <- c()
  for (g in c("A", "B", "C")) {
    reg <- if(g == "A") "#FF0000" else if(g == "B") "#0000FF" else "#00FF00"
    light <- if(g == "A") "#FF9999" else if(g == "B") "#9999FF" else "#99FF99"
    color_palette[paste(g, "Enriched_in_-2", sep = "_")] <- reg
    color_palette[paste(g, "Decreased_in_-2", sep = "_")] <- light
  }
  
  # Create plot with legend in 2 columns
  p <- ggplot(combined_data, aes(x = Species, y = Mean_Coeff, fill = FillGroup)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), width = 0.8) +
    geom_text(aes(label = signif,
                  y = ifelse(Mean_Coeff >= 0,
                             Mean_Coeff + 0.1 * max(abs(combined_data$Mean_Coeff)),
                             Mean_Coeff - 0.1 * max(abs(combined_data$Mean_Coeff)))),
              position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), size = 3, vjust = 0.5) +
    scale_fill_manual(
      name = "Group & Direction",
      values = color_palette,
      labels = c("A_Enriched_in_-2" = "Group A (enriched in -2)",
                 "A_Decreased_in_-2" = "Group A (decreased in -2)",
                 "B_Enriched_in_-2" = "Group B (enriched in -2)",
                 "B_Decreased_in_-2" = "Group B (decreased in -2)",
                 "C_Enriched_in_-2" = "Group C (enriched in -2)",
                 "C_Decreased_in_-2" = "Group C (decreased in -2)")
    ) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
    coord_flip() +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      axis.text.y = element_text(face = "italic", size = 10, colour = 'black'),
      axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12, colour = 'black'),
      axis.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
      plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
      legend.position = "top",
      legend.text = element_text(size = 12),
      legend.title = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold"),
      legend.box = "horizontal",
      plot.margin = margin(1, 1, 1, 1, "cm")
    ) +
    guides(fill = guide_legend(ncol = 2, byrow = TRUE)) +
    labs(
      title = paste("Timepoint comparisons (-2 vs -1) – Global (NEGBIN TMM)"),
      x = "Pathways",
      y = "Coefficient (Timepoint -2 vs -1)\n(positive = enriched in -2, negative = decreased in -2)"
    )
  
  # Save plot with increased width
  n_species <- length(unique(combined_data$Species))
  plot_height <- max(8, n_species * 0.4)
  plot_width <- 18
  
  ggsave(file.path(output_dir, paste0("plot_", filename, "_combined.pdf")),
         p, width = plot_width, height = plot_height, limitsize = FALSE)
  ggsave(file.path(output_dir, paste0("plot_", filename, "_combined.png")),
         p, width = plot_width, height = plot_height, dpi = 300, limitsize = FALSE)
  
  cat("        ✓ Combined plot saved with", n_species, "species (", nrow(combined_data), "bars)\n")
  return(p)
}

# ============================================================================
# SECTION 5: MAIN ANALYSIS – NEGBIN TMM FOR ALL GROUPS
# ============================================================================

cat("\n", strrep("=", 60), "\n")
cat("Model: NEGBIN + TMM\n")
cat(strrep("=", 60), "\n")

# Use the raw data for NEGBIN
data_all <- data_raw
metadata_all <- metadf

cat("  Total samples:", ncol(data_all), "\n")

# Collect results per group (full significant features)
group_results_full <- list()

# Open debug file for detailed output
sink(debug_file, append = FALSE)  # Overwrite previous debug file
cat("=== DETAILED DEBUG OUTPUT FOR EACH GROUP (before per‑group top‑20) ===\n\n")
sink()  # Back to console and main log

for (group in groups) {
  cat("\n  Group:", group, "\n")
  idx_group <- which(metadata_all$Group == group)
  n_group <- length(idx_group)
  cat("    Samples in group:", n_group, "\n")
  if (n_group < min_samples_per_timepoint * 2) {
    cat("    Insufficient samples for group (<", min_samples_per_timepoint*2, "). Skipping.\n")
    next
  }
  
  # Check timepoint distribution
  timepoint_counts <- table(metadata_all$Timepoint[idx_group])
  cat("    Timepoint distribution: -1 =", timepoint_counts["-1"], ", -2 =", timepoint_counts["-2"], "\n")
  if (any(timepoint_counts < min_samples_per_timepoint)) {
    cat("    Insufficient samples in one timepoint. Skipping.\n")
    next
  }
  
  # Subset data and metadata for this group
  data_group <- data_all[, idx_group, drop = FALSE]
  metadata_group <- metadata_all[idx_group, , drop = FALSE]
  
  # Remove features with zero total count in this subset
  non_zero <- rowSums(data_group) > 0
  data_group <- data_group[non_zero, , drop = FALSE]
  cat("    Features after zero removal:", nrow(data_group), "\n")
  
  if (nrow(data_group) == 0) {
    cat("    No features remain after zero removal. Skipping.\n")
    next
  }
  
  # Run MaAsLin2
  output_prefix <- paste("group", group, "NEGBIN_TMM", sep = "_")
  res <- run_maaslin_model(data_group, metadata_group, output_prefix, debug = TRUE)
  if (!is.null(res)) {
    group_results_full[[group]] <- res
    cat("    Found", nrow(res), "significant species (q ≤ 0.05, |coef| ≥ 1)\n")
  } else {
    cat("    No significant species found\n")
  }
}

# ============================================================================
# SECTION 6: PER‑GROUP TOP 20 SELECTION
# ============================================================================

cat("\n", strrep("=", 60), "\n")
cat("Applying per‑group top‑20 selection (positive and negative separately)...\n")
cat(strrep("=", 60), "\n")

# Collect selected species after per‑group top‑20
selected_species_all <- character(0)

for (group in groups) {
  if (!is.null(group_results_full[[group]])) {
    df <- group_results_full[[group]]
    
    # Separate positive and negative
    pos <- df %>% filter(Mean_Coeff > 0)
    neg <- df %>% filter(Mean_Coeff < 0)
    
    # Take top 20 positive (by coefficient descending)
    if (nrow(pos) > 20) {
      pos_top <- pos %>% arrange(desc(Mean_Coeff)) %>% slice_head(n = 20)
    } else {
      pos_top <- pos
    }
    
    # Take top 20 negative (by coefficient ascending, i.e., most negative)
    if (nrow(neg) > 20) {
      neg_top <- neg %>% arrange(Mean_Coeff) %>% slice_head(n = 20)
    } else {
      neg_top <- neg
    }
    
    # Combine selected species for this group
    selected_species <- c(pos_top$Species, neg_top$Species)
    selected_species_all <- c(selected_species_all, selected_species)
    
    cat("Group", group, ": selected", nrow(pos_top), "positive and", nrow(neg_top), "negative species\n")
    
    # Write detailed selection to debug file
    sink(debug_file, append = TRUE)
    cat("\n=== Group", group, " – Selected for plot ===\n")
    cat("Positive (top 20):\n")
    if (nrow(pos_top) > 0) print(pos_top[, c("Species", "Mean_Coeff")])
    cat("\nNegative (top 20):\n")
    if (nrow(neg_top) > 0) print(neg_top[, c("Species", "Mean_Coeff")])
    sink()
  } else {
    cat("Group", group, ": no significant features\n")
  }
}

# Keep only selected species in the combined data
selected_species_all <- unique(selected_species_all)
combined_data <- bind_rows(group_results_full) %>%
  filter(Species %in% selected_species_all)

cat("\nTotal selected species across all groups:", length(unique(combined_data$Species)), "\n")

# Write combined summary to debug file
sink(debug_file, append = TRUE)
cat("\n=== COMBINED DATA AFTER PER‑GROUP SELECTION ===\n")
cat("Total rows:", nrow(combined_data), "\n")
cat("Per group:\n")
print(table(combined_data$Group))
cat("Per direction:\n")
print(table(combined_data$Direction))
cat("Per group & direction:\n")
print(table(combined_data$Group, combined_data$Direction))
sink()

# ============================================================================
# SECTION 7: CREATE COMBINED PLOT
# ============================================================================

if (nrow(combined_data) > 0) {
  clean_name <- "global_NEGBIN_TMM_per_group_top20"
  title <- "Global Analysis - NEGBIN TMM (per-group top 20)"
  create_combined_plot(combined_data, title, output_dir, clean_name,
                       group_colors = c("A" = "#FF0000", "B" = "#0000FF", "C" = "#00FF00"),
                       group_colors_light = c("A" = "#FF9999", "B" = "#9999FF", "C" = "#99FF99"))
} else {
  cat("\n  No data to plot.\n")
}

cat("\nAnalysis completed at:", as.character(Sys.time()), "\n")
sink()
```
